programme.desc: A petty officer is killed while competing in a swimsuit contest, raising fears she may have fallen victim to a jealous rival, or even a stalker. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An officer is found dead, and evidence initially suggests she committed suicide as a result of a nervous breakdown. However, a sceptical Ducky believes foul play was involved - and the team later discovers the victim was caught up in a love triangle. Kevin Quigley guest stars.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine is murdered, but it is not long before the prime suspect is also found dead. Tim and Abby discover a clue to both killings in an e-mail that suggests the victims were involved in a game with deadly consequences. Drama, starring Sean Murray and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine suspected of murdering a girl is killed in Iraq, and a search of his personal belongings reveals her body. Most members of the team believe they have found conclusive evidence of his guilt - but Gibbs suspects there is more to the situation than meets the eye.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is infected with a deadly virus sent through the post by shadowy figures who demand the details of a long-closed case be made public in exchange for the antidote. McGee and Gibbs set out to identify the culprits and find a cure for the disease. Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon star.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is hired by the Department of Defense to break into Fort Knox to test their security - but when a sinister discovery is made, the group is forced to find a way into the complex again. Drama inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is employed to install a concrete sarcophagus at the site of the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant before further radiation leaks occur.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on a joint Coast Guard and NCIS training exercise at sea with Agent Borin, Gibbs spots a dead man overboard, who is found to be a missing member of the crew of a nearby vessel. However, when the agent boards the ship for questioning, he realises it has been commandeered by pirates. Naval drama, starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a Navy commander's wife, determining whether it is linked to her role as a therapist, or an act of terrorism since her husband's name has appeared on a Jihadist's target list. Elsewhere, the group discusses Halloween costumes, as well as Tony's recent dating habits. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers a fraudulent charity that preys on those searching for missing military personnel while investigating the murder of a retired Master Sergeant. Meanwhile, Bishop agonises over the results of her probation evaluation, and Tony contemplates adopting a stray cat that followed him home. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the cause of a hit-and-run incident in which a Petty Officer has been killed. Meanwhile, Gibbs works to clear a Navy Hospital Corpsman who has been indicted for illegally providing medical aid to victims of the crash, despite the injured insisting her techniques helped save their lives. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An enigmatic killer shoots dead a businessman spending Thanksgiving at a hotel in the city, then takes his glasses, jewellery and ID to Washington Dulles airport, which has been brought to a stand-still by a massive snowstorm. As the murderer's actions take effect, a terror alert is issued, leaving all northern US airports in a state of panic, and leaving it up to DiNozzo, Bishop and her husband - all of whom are trapped at Dulles by the weather - to bring the armed villain to justice. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley.(n)

programme.desc: The investigators examine the death of US marine Tommy Hill, who appears to have been fatally stabbed en route to a homecoming party thrown in his honour by his foster family. McGee and Ziva follow a trail of blood that leads them to a mobile phone, while Abby discovers the victim had been trying to hack into Virginia's state website and Ducky's autopsy reveals that Hill had been injected with a sedative.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray.(n)

programme.title: Doctor Finlay

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Highland medic Dr Finlay begins to suspect the Reverend Gill may be more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next, and sets out to investigate. Meanwhile, his colleague Dr Napier delivers glad tidings to a married woman - but does not receive the joyful response he anticipated. Rural drama, with David Rintoul and Jessica Turner.(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A dawn raid by bailiffs leaves the antiques dealer staring ruin in the face, and with nothing left to look forward to, he decides to turn his back on society. With Ian McShane and guest star Alexei Sayle.(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The dealer recruits con artists Matron Jackie, Gideon and Henry the Hearse Chaser to attend an auction where he is selling a painting, to purposely bid against each other to bump up the price. However, Lady Jane also bids and after discovering the ruse concocts a plan to get her own back and wipe the smile off Lovejoy's face. Charlotte Cornwell guest stars with Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan.(n)

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Obadiah Hakeswill returns to create more mischief, kidnapping a beautiful aristocrat and undermining the discipline of Wellesley's men. Sharpe is dispatched to thwart his old foe, and manages to inflict a humiliating defeat on French forces in the process - but only at terrible personal loss to himself. Sean Bean, Elizabeth Hurley and Pete Postlethwaite star.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Ally's dodgy dealings take a turn for the worse, and Barry has difficulty controlling Hazel who seems to be suffering from an overdose of sun, sea and sangria - to say nothing of gambling fever. Acclaimed comedy drama, starring Bill Paterson, Timothy Spall and Melanie Hill.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Barry finally announces his long-awaited wedding to Hazel is definitely going ahead and Oz secretly embarks on a romance of his own. Needless to say, Ally is concocting another dodgy scheme in the background and, as he puts it into operation, the lads sail off over the horizon. Comedy drama, starring Jimmy Nail, Timothy Spall and Tim Healy.(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.sub-title: The Memory of Water - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. The green-fingered amateur sleuths are drawn into another mystery when a barrister employing them to spruce up his garden discovers his cousin has been drowned, only for the man to be spotted alive and well a few days later. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star.(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.sub-title: The Memory of Water - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The amateur sleuths continue their investigation after Martin Frazer's cousin, who supposedly drowned, is spotted alive and well a few days later. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris.(n)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are enlisted to design a memorial garden based on the myth of Orpheus and Eurydice, but someone begins vandalising their work on a nightly basis. Matters are complicated still further by the death of a recovering alcoholic, whose body is found at the site. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris, with Phyllida Law, Joanna David and Daisy Dunlop.(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The owner and curator of Brokenwood's Historic Village, Charity Highmore-Browne, dies after being struck by an arrow. Mike and his team delve into the realm of New Zealand's colonial past as they deal with suspects including a blacksmith, a missionary, the local postmaster and the victim's sister. Neill Rea stars.(n)

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: When a man dies in a deliberately set fire, Murdoch's investigation into the aftermath reveals some shocking personal history.(n)

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: A charity fundraiser who has travelled the country in a bath of baked beans is shot in a robbery, but his obsessive compulsive disorder gets on Gene's nerves, causing the DCI to beat a confession out of anyone who gets in his way. After the Clown warns Alex someone is going to die, she worries about Hunt's strong-arm tactics - and fights to keep control of the case. Matthew Macfadyen guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Alex realises there are just 24 hours until her parents die in a car bombing and chooses a horrified Ray to help her save them. Amid the chaos of a Gay Pride march, she arrests Tim and Caroline in the hope of keeping them safe, but with Lord Scarman due to visit the station a nervous Gene releases all the prisoners, aiming to make a good impression. Guest starring Geoffrey Palmer.(n)

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: The parents of a teenage murder victim ask Kavanagh to pursue a private prosecution on their behalf and he promptly agrees, despite the fact they are unemployed and unable to pay a fee. The accused is related to a powerful local crook, and finding witnesses prepared to testify proves no easy task. John Thaw stars, with Lisa Harrow and Nicholas Jones.(n)

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Fifteen-year-old Cassie Booth goes missing while doing her local paper round, and her blood-stained clothes are later found in a nearby boathouse owned by Karl Wilding. He is the prime suspect, but then seemingly helpful Stephen Warrington, a wine merchant who has initially brought a complaint about a house being used as a brothel, demonstrates too much interest in the case.(n)

programme.title: Garden Hopping
programme.desc: Daphne Ledward visits the stunning Rowden Gardens on Dartmoor - one of the participants in the National Gardens Scheme.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: One Step Too Far - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Laura looks at Gabriel with new eyes when he takes a surprisingly compassionate approach to her wayward son Liam, who is being bullied because of her job. Adam tries to mend fences with the rank-and-file officers by going out on the beat with June - only to lose his cool completely when a traffic accident triggers painful memories. Samantha jeopardises Jo's investigation by poaching her informant, leading to a bitter row, while Gary's future on the force looks uncertain after he reacts to Suzie's constant needling with a very public outburst.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: One Step Too Far - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Laura begins to feel increasingly unfulfilled in her role as PCSO, and announces to Smithy that she wants to become a PC. Gina's old flame Jonathan Fox returns to Sun Hill, and Neil pressures Gary to drop his bullying accusations against Susie Sim. Gabriel warns an increasingly infatuated Sheelagh not to get involved with him, while Samantha continues to needle Jo, fuelling an already volatile situation.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Zain Nadir searches for a missing 14-year-old whose online romance has led her into danger, and a guilt-stricken Gabriel reveals to Sheelagh that he deliberately killed the Sun Hill sniper by pushing him from a rooftop. Meanwhile, Honey returns to work after the shock suicide of murderous husband Scott, and finds herself paired with arch-enemy Amber Johannsen. True to form, it isn't long before they come to blows.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Zain Nadir discovers Smithy has been canoodling with the wife of notorious local gangster Pete Larson, while arch-rivals Amber and Honey are teamed up yet again, this time to catch a gang defrauding cash machines. June has another bash at warning Sheelagh against getting too involved with Gabriel, and Phil is livid to discover ex-lover Christine is planning on emigrating to Australia - taking their daughter Madison with her.(n)

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Detective Inspector Henry Crabbe prepares for his retirement and the opening of his restaurant - but the police force seems reluctant to let him go. First episode of the crime drama, starring Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed.(n)

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Henry is enlisted in the search for a missing policeman, but the restaurant's opening night is looming. Detective drama, starring Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel, starring Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha. A wealthy eligible bachelor rents a property in Hertfordshire, causing a flurry of anticipation among the ladies of the district. However, it transpires he also brought along a friend - the haughty and aloof Fitzwilliam Darcy.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Mrs Bennet spots an opportunity to marry off another of her daughters with the arrival of clergyman Mr Collins. However, Elizabeth is unable to take his proposal seriously, being too preoccupied with a handsome newcomer who poisons her mind further against Mr Darcy. Alison Steadman, Jennifer Ehle and David Bamber star in Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Elizabeth visits Mr Collins once Charlotte has accepted his proposal of marriage and confides in Jane that she regards him as one of the stupidest men in England. Mrs Bennet sizes up George Wickham as a potential suitor for her daughter - but he has set his heart on society heiress Miss King.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Rejected by Elizabeth, Darcy decides to return to Rosling Park - and resolves to reveal the truth about Wickham's character in a letter. Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha star.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Despite Miss Bingley's efforts to keep them apart, Darcy and Elizabeth's relationship grows more affectionate - until bad news arrives from Longbourn about Lydia and Wickham. Jane Austen adaptation, starring Jennifer Ehle, Colin Firth and Julia Sawalha.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Wickham's elopement with Lydia makes Elizabeth see him for what he really is, while Bingley proposes to Jane - an offer she readily accepts. Colin Firth and Jennifer Ehle star.(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives return home after their trip to South Africa, to find Nonnatus House has come under new management. The formidable Sister Ursula (Harriet Walter) has taken charge and introduced some drastic changes to the way the clinic is run, which Sister Julienne, bound by her vow of obedience, is powerless to oppose. The midwives come to the aid of a victim of domestic violence whose husband is about to be released from prison, and who risks losing everything if she seeks a divorce. Sister Mary Cynthia suffers further anxiety attacks, and Patsy receives distressing news about her father.(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The owner and curator of Brokenwood's Historic Village, Charity Highmore-Browne, dies after being struck by an arrow. Neill Rea stars.(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Two long-dead bodies are found in a disused coal mine on the outskirts of Glasgow when it is reopened to serve as the central attraction for a museum. Investigations reveal the pair had been involved in violent encounters just before their deaths, and the search to discover their identities uncovers deeply buried resentments in the community. Detective drama, starring Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie.(n)

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Pat is forced to deal with a persistent complainer who has been hounding the station, and becomes concerned when he starts stalking her. Eventually Pat is abducted by Warrington and forced to discover Cassie North's corpse. Drama, starring Kate Buffery and Richard E Grant.(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The dealer recruits con artists Matron Jackie, Gideon and Henry the Hearse Chaser to attend an auction where he is selling a painting, to purposely bid against each other to bump up the price. However, Lady Jane also bids and after discovering the ruse concocts a plan to get her own back and wipe the smile off Lovejoy's face. Charlotte Cornwell guest stars with Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan.(n)

programme.title: Garden Hopping
programme.desc: Daphne Ledward visits the inspirational Bluebell Cottage in Cheshire, which is open to the public as part of the National Gardens Scheme project.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah performs a spiritual cleansing in the local creek as a religious occasion approaches, but she's spotted by Doris Collins. She finds it hard to deal with the woman's reaction to her nudity, but it is her own personal demons that she is struggling with the most. Marta Dusseldorp and Deborah Kennedy star.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Anna and Gino's romance continues to blossom as they sneak off to spend time together - even though it's clear that something about their secret trysts is playing on Gino's mind. Meanwhile, at Ash Park, Olivia and James's relationship is at breaking point as Olivia struggles with the identity of James's lover. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry appears in court to testify against brother Ben, but reacts badly to being questioned about his own childhood abuse, indirectly jeopardising the outcome of the trial. Phil begins to realise he is powerless to stop former lover Christine emigrating with their daughter Madison, while Smithy spends the night with sultry Louise Larson, unaware Zain Nadir is investigating the criminal activities of her husband Pete.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Connie is devastated when her patient collapses and there is not enough time to arrange a heart transplant. Nick tries to get Diane on his side by letting her perform a difficult procedure for the first time, while Ric talks her into taking the research position full time. Kyla brings Max to work as he is a diabetic and she is worried about his blood sugar levels.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: The atmosphere is tense between new nurse Barney Wolfe and Adam as they fail to see eye to eye. Two serious cases of food poisoning panic one unfortunate fellow, who rushes home to check on his son, only to discover that his wife is having an affair, and Duffy begins to feel the pressures of her new responsibilities. Derek Thompson, Cathy Shipton and Ian Bleasdale star.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Anita Dobson guests as Phil Hunter's mum Lynne, who turns up at the station to lecture him on his responsibilities as a father-to-be. Smithy seizes an opportunity to meet local villain's wife Louise Larson and continue their dangerous relationship, while Adam struggles to make headway with his road safety campaign, until old friend Isaac Collins pulls a few strings at the local council to help get it under way. In court, the case against Terry's brother Ben concludes - but the outcome promises to have a devastating long-term effect on the entire family.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Shelley's ready to be more than friends with Mark. Pauline's determined to find out about Pete's disappearance. Gita warns Cindy about Richard.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Richard continues to pursue Cindy, meanwhile Pauline has concerns about Arthur.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: A man climbs a chimney in protest against the Thames Relief Road, but the rescue escalates into a serious operation.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Archie begins coaching the Glenbogle five-a-side team for their big match against Glencampbell, but reluctantly hands over the reins to his brother Paul to allow him to concentrate on important estate business. Molly attempts to avoid a weekend trip with Andrew, fearing she is not ready for a new romance, and Golly is reunited with his estranged daughter Jess.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The nurses get more than they bargained for as they prepare for a darts match in the Skelthwaite Arms, and Beth has a surprise visitor. Drama, starring Lesley Dunlop and Leslie Ash.(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: The Boswell family try to cope without Nellie, who has some surprise visitors - her 'fancy man' Derek and Lilo Lill.(n)

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: It is a day of discontent at Maplin's when the pay packets arrive, and Ted and Spike act out the French Revolution in the swimming pool. Comedy, starring Paul Shane, David Griffin and Ruth Madoc.(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean and Lionel settle down to enjoy a quiet evening, only to find themselves constantly disturbed by ominous noises from the empty house next door. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo gets out his old motorbike and tries to perform a few stunts - much to the surprise of Blamire and Clegg. Bill Owen, Michael Bates and Peter Sallis star.(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective duo are called to a hospital, where a series of suspicious deaths has raised fears a serial killer may be at work on the wards. A grisly collection of body parts is found in a nearby lake, and both patients and staff are dropping dead with alarming regularity. However, Pascoe soon finds his own health is as much of a worry as the case itself. Robert Powell, Miriam Karlin and Haydn Gwynne guest star.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Fresh DNA tests on an unidentified body found in 1996 reveal a family link to a Home Office fingerprint analyst, who has not seen her brother since she was seven, when he brought her over to the UK from their home country of Albania. The victim had been working as a police interpreter - and the motive seems clear when it turns out his last assignment was translating for a witness in a murder trial against a notorious Albanian criminal. Meanwhile, Gerry embarks on a French cookery course, but the language barrier results in a rather surprising date. Amanda Redman and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Eve links DNA evidence found at a traffic collision to a murder case she worked on while examining mass graves during the Balkan War, leaving Boyd determined to track down the perpetrators, a ring of war criminals now living and working in the UK. Starring Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston, with guest stars Ron Cook, Anna Madeley and Branka Katic.(n)

programme.title: The Damned United (2009)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama chronicling Brian Clough's ill-fated 44-day tenure as boss of Leeds United in 1974, after Don Revie's departure for the job of England's manager. Following his acrimonious split from Derby County and a brief spell at Brighton & Hove Albion, Clough arrived at Elland Road on a wave of controversy, after previously being an outspoken critic of the club, and struggled to find allies among the playing staff. With Michael Sheen, Timothy Spall, Jim Broadbent and Colm Meaney.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Four weeks into the Government's lockdown to save lives and protect the NHS, Jane Corbin reports from the frontline to tell the inside story of how a Coventry hospital is coping with Covid-19. She hears from doctors and nurses saving lives and dealing with death every day and asks whether there is enough protective equipment and testing to help protect them from the daily risks to their own health.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany berates Keegan for his bad mood and the chaos at the Taylors' only adds to his stress, leading to an angry outburst - but he is mortified to realise Bailey has witnessed his violent. Sonia urges Whitney to get back to normal, but that proves easier said than done with Michaela posting disagreeable online comments online, leaving her crushed. Ash cries on Vinny's shoulder but they both panic when Suki arrives home early, and after speaking to Callum, Stuart is inspired to propose to Rainie again.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Bake Off judge Prue Leith, reality TV star Scarlett Moffatt, rapper Big Narstie and weatherman Tomasz Schafernaker join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A student backpacker is found dead in the showers of her hostel, and with a gun in her hand and her body slumped against the only door, all evidence points toward suicide. Humphrey isn't so sure, however, especially when he notices all her toiletries laid out and her towel missing. But when the team question the victim's boyfriend and her fellow backpackers, they find themselves with very little to go on. Meanwhile, JP prepares for the biggest day of his life as his wedding day approaches, and when Humphrey bumps into an old lady friend, Florence encourages her boss to pursue his quest for love.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team return to Mexico to search for more dangerous creatures, including a crocodile with a nasty bite and one of the world's most vicious sharks.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores dangerous fauna in Venezuela, including green anacondas, vampire bats and scolopendras - giant venomous centipedes.(n)

programme.title: Astronauts: Do You Have What It Takes?
programme.desc: The remaining candidates are put through more demanding tests by astronaut Chris Hadfield and his expert panel, with some pushed to their limits in a simulated emergency splashdown, where they have to escape from an underwater capsule. At one of the UK's biggest space-tech firms, they must successfully operate the Mars Rover, while a social event gives Chris, Kevin Fong and Iya Whiteley a further chance to scrutinise their characters.(n)

programme.title: Operation Wild
programme.desc: Broadcaster Clare Balding and vet Steve Leonard travel the world to watch vets perform innovative life-saving operations on a variety of wild animals. This first edition features a baby panda called Zhu Xia in south-west China in need of an MRI scan, and rescued gorilla Shufai in Cameroon, who was shot by poachers when he was a baby. Plus, a manta ray on the Japanese island of Okinawa is given an ultra-sound scan to determine if it's pregnant, and a four-ton elephant in Laos with a gunshot wound.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Tom Barnes, Sam Buckley, Hrishikesh Desai and George Farrugia compete in the heats for the North West. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature, specifically tasked with honouring the theme of action and adventure. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs from the North West cook mains and desserts celebrating children's literature, and while one takes inspiration from JM Barrie and his own imaginary feast, the other two choose Beatrix Potter, leaving judge Michael O'Hare to decide which lamb dish is better.(n)

programme.title: The Â£100K House: Tricks of the Trade
programme.desc: Desperate to continue living in London, Adam bought one of the thinnest homes in the country. However, at six foot wide in places, the former alleyway was dark and barely habitable. Architect Katy Marks helps to transform his home with radical ideas, and Piers Taylor demonstrates how lighting, furniture and colours can make all the difference. Meanwhile, in Walsall, Hardeep and Rupinder have plenty of room for their family, but in all the wrong places. Piers Taylor designs them the ultimate social space, and encourages them to orientate their home around the garden.(n)

programme.title: World Snooker: Crucible Classics
programme.desc: Steve Davis v Tony Knowles. Hazel Irvine introduces action from the first-round match at the 1982 World Championship, staged at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield.(n)

programme.title: Talking Snooker
programme.desc: Alistair McGowan takes an affectionate look at the lives and careers of Alex Higgins, Jimmy White and Ronnie O'Sullivan, three of snooker's biggest stars. He examines the early TV appearances that captured each of them before they hit the big time, and charts the ups and downs of their respective careers, in an effort to capture a sense of why they were all known as the people's champion.(n)

programme.title: Porridge (1979)
programme.desc: Cellmates Fletcher and Godber are coerced into aiding an escape attempt organised by the sinister Grouty. However, the two jailbirds contrive to break back into prison before their absence is detected by the formidable Mr Mackay. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale, Peter Vaughan, Fulton Mackay and Brian Wilde.(n)

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.sub-title: The Many Faces of Ronnie Barker
programme.desc: The story of a quiet, dedicated man who might have been a bank manager, but went on to become one of the nation's most popular comedy stars. Ben Elton, Michael Grade, David Renwick and Josephine Tewson are among friends and colleagues who remember his partnership with Ronnie Corbett in sketch show The Two Ronnies, as skinflint grocer Arkwright in Open All Hours and crafty prisoner Fletch in Porridge. There is also a look at his prolific writing career and his work as an actor in serious roles.(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits the Thai capital of Bangkok, where he learns how the late 19th-century King Rama V built a grand palace and introduced railways to the country. Michael also learns that King Rama V's love of steam engines was inspired by Queen Victoria. Moving on, the presenter also confronts his fear of snakes to assist in a surgical operation on a venomous king cobra and steps into the ring to learn Muay Thai boxing.(n)

programme.title: The Sense of an Ending (2017)
programme.desc: Divorced and retired, aging Londoner and vintage camera shop owner Tony Webster whittles down the solitude of his isolated existence by keeping an affectionate relationship with his ex-wife, Margaret, and by accompanying his pregnant daughter, Susie, to antenatal courses. However, the unexpected arrival of an unsettling letter disrupts the fine balance of things in Tony's orderly life, reconnecting him with his first love from college, Veronica, and the nostalgic, yet clouded memories of a distant past. Drama, starring Jim Broadbent, Charlotte Rampling and Harriet Walter.(n)

programme.title: The Conversation (1974)
programme.desc: An obsessive, paranoid electronic surveillance expert - renowned within the profession as being the best - makes the unprofessional mistake of getting personally involved in a dangerous case on his San Francisco patch. Francis Ford Coppola's thriller, starring Gene Hackman, John Cazale, Robert Duvall, Allen Garfield and Harrison Ford.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The presenters pick out their favourite films from the programme's archive, including Anita Rani surfing in Snowdonia, Ellie Harrison counting gannets, Matt Baker visiting an alpaca blood bank and Helen Skelton going fell running. Plus, Adam Henson is on his farm finding out about the pecking order of his chickens.(n)

programme.title: Heavenly Gardens with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: Part two of two. Alexander and Arit Anderson visit gardens expressing hope, joy and renewal. At the Bishop's Palace in Wells, Somerset, Alexander discovers why this place has been sacred for centuries, and helps to create a colourful and uplifting work of art. Arit learns how the ancient trees at Scone Palace in Perthshire will be given a new lease of life, and Alexander heads to his home county of Northumberland to tour a garden using humour to plant seeds of change in the community.(n)

programme.title: The British Garden: Life and Death on Your Lawn
programme.desc: Chris Packham and a group of experts spend 12 months scrutinising the wildlife in eight gardens on a suburban street in Welwyn Garden City. Throughout the seasons they find male crickets that bribe females with food during sex, spiders that change colour to help catch prey, and ferocious life-and-death battles in the compost heap. They also shows a different side to familiar garden residents, revealing how a robin's red breast is actually war paint, and a single litter of foxes can have up to five different fathers.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: For the first time ever, the Beechgrove presenters give weekly updates from their own patches of Scotland, beginning with Carole Baxter in Aberdeenshire.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest scoring chefs from the North West go head to head to cook their six-course menus again. Not only do they need to impress food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver, but also bestselling children's author and Laureate between 2011 and 2013 Julia Donaldson, who has sold over 65 million books worldwide and been translated into over 75 languages. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their celebration of British food. The culinary duo share fascinating stories as they explore the festivals and occasions that allow the nation to put on a spread and indulge.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison use virtual reality to redesign a 1930s Surrey house with a poorly designed layout to make better use of the space, and allow the owners to both start a family and have their parents move in with them in the future. Laura's plan involves turning the property into a light-filled interconnected series of spaces, while Robert turns conventional wisdom on its head with a visually striking and flexible design. After experiencing both potential houses, the owners must choose which they would like to put into practice.(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall's tour of the West Country continues as he explores the Forest of Dean. He enlists the aid of local wildlife enthusiasts to track down imposing wild boar, which have returned to the area for the first time in 300 years. He also has a close encounter with a goshawk, a bird of prey that specialises in hunting in woodland, and one that has come to be known locally as 'the phantom of the forest'.(n)

programme.title: World Snooker: Crucible Classics
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine introduces action from the 1982 World Championship final between Ray Reardon and Alex Higgins, staged at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield.(n)

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The People's Champion
programme.desc: James Nesbitt narrates a look back at the often controversial life of the two-time world snooker champion, who was widely regarded as one of the most naturally gifted players in the history of his sport, but whose brilliance was flawed by his demons. With contributions by members of his family, as well as fellow snooker greats Jimmy White, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Steve Davis and Dennis Taylor.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a foundry in France that produces iron pots and pans at the rate of one every five seconds, while Cherry Healey is in South Africa, at one of the world's largest iron ore mines, and rides in a giant dumper truck. Historian Ruth Goodman explains how cooking in pots has evolved, from communal ovens to 1970s slow cookers.(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across Central America, from the Caribbean to the Pacific. Along the way, he goes diving on a coral reef on the coast of Belize, and witnesses a gun battle between park rangers and intruders in Chiquibul National Park. He also discovers how climate change has left Guatemala devastated by famine and drought, reports on measures to curb gang violence in El Salvador, and learns how Costa Rica aims to become the world's first carbon neutral country by 2021.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: After a frantic race to make it to Ilha Grande, the teams set off for the next checkpoint in Mendoza, Western Argentina, but as the race enters its final phase, the depleted budgets begin to take their toll on the competitors. Emon and Jamiul are the first to set off, but disagree about the best route to take. Jen and Rob are the team with the most money remaining, but also have clashing priorities over how best to proceed. Jo and Sam look for jobs that include bed and board to save their money, while Dom and Lizzie hitch a ride and receive an unexpected invitation.(n)

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.sub-title: Stacey Dooley: Locked Up With the Lifers
programme.desc: The presenter visits a women's prison in America that has adopted a radical new approach to incarceration based on giving the inmates a sense of purpose and self-esteem. She meets eight prisoners who are serving life sentences for murder, and discovers the complex sexual relationships that have developed among many of the inmates.(n)

programme.title: Trigonometry
programme.desc: Gemma is running out of options to save the cafe, but when Ray comes up with a solution, it highlights a problem in their relationship with Gemma. The three come to the conclusion that they have to tell their families the truth about their relationship, a decision which has unforeseen consequences for all the people in their lives - but leaves them determined to make an even bigger commitment.(n)

programme.title: Limmy's Homemade Show
programme.desc: Rough-and-ready self-made comedy show featuring Scottish comedian Limmy, aka Brian Limond. Limmy reveals his secret life as a hitman, gives sneaky advice to youngsters and explains why he's paranoid about hanging his minging towel on the washing line. Plus, a businessman has a crisis of confidence when he accidentally goes to put his kettle in the fridge.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: Sitcom spin-off from the movie in which a documentary crew follow the lives of a group of vampire housemates in New York. Ottoman warlord Nandor, British dandy Laszlo, seductive Nadja, tedious 'energy vampire' Colin, and human servant Guillermo receive a visit from their dark lord, who demands to know why they have yet to conquer the United States. Starring Matt Berry, Kayvan Novak and Natasia Demetriou, and written and directed by the original movie's stars Taika Waititi and Jemaine Clement.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The vampires get involved in Staten Island local politics as their first step on the road to world domination. Starring Matt Berry, Kayvan Novak and Natasia Demetriou.(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week.(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The investigators look into an IT support worker shamelessly helping himself to hospital computers and a gang illegally fly-tipping on an industrial scale.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Xavier is determined to prove that Cameron killed Evan as the day of the disciplinary hearing dawns, but he may not be able to gather enough evidence to clear his name. Essie faces her first round of chemotherapy, and Sacha begs her to tell her family and friends the truth. Nicky is given the opportunity to carry out a challenging heart transplant under Jac's supervision.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of the army of night workers. Here, the team at one of the country's biggest shopping malls installs a three-metre tall reindeer for Christmas shoppers. In Tynemouth the biggest fish catch of the year is landed with only hours to process the catch, while refuse collectors work hard to get Newcastle's streets clean and waste recycled before dawn breaks.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The remaining 16 contestants are split into two groups as they compete for the chance of their first taste of working in a professional kitchen. The first eight cook a dish for John Torode and Gregg Wallace, with the best five heading to Da Terra in east London to work under chef Rafael Cagali. During a busy lunch service, the amateurs will be entirely responsible for serving the restaurant's menu to paying customers. Will any buckle under the intense pressure as they cook in a demanding new environment?(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: Colin Salmon narrates a documentary exploring how the lives of humans and animals across a vast area of the planet are affected by the transforming power of the monsoon rains. The end of the dry season in northern Australia sees 300,000 flying foxes gathering to drink while running a gauntlet of freshwater crocodiles, while in Sumatra a female orang-utan guides her youngster to where she knows a fig tree fruits at this time of year. At the Delhi stock exchange, the price of rice escalates every day the rains fail to materialise and priests submerge themselves in water and beseech Varuna, god of water, to come.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Ian and Toby from Essex and Charley and David also from Essex compete for a chance to meet today's curious collector Charlie. With Â£1,000 at stake in the Curiosity Shop, who will curate the most valuable collection? Paul Martin hosts.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bakers
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King travel the country to examine the UK's obsession with teatime treats. They make Bakewell tart in its original Derbyshire home and prepare a Victoria sandwich for the ladies of the WI. At Henley regatta, the duo prepare cookies with sour cherries and chocolate chips, and later serve afternoon tea to the Tees Rowing Club.(n)

programme.title: World Snooker: Crucible Classics
programme.desc: Steve Davis v Jimmy White. Hazel Irvine introduces action from the 1984 World Championship final, staged at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Chef Ainsley Harriott takes Len Goodman on a road trip around Britain to expand the Strictly Come Dancing judge's culinary horizons. The pair begin in Manchester, where Len has his first taste of Chinese food and samples some Mexican mezcal, while Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a stir-fried beef dish inspired by the day's adventures.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts David Harper and Claire Rawle search for items to sell at auction at Penrhyn Castle in north Wales. Presented by Paul Martin.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Amanda Abbington, Michael Buerk, Shappi Khorsandi and Elis James test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Poultry farmers Nick and Jacob and organic and heritage fruit and vegetable farmer Patrick display their wares at Alexandra Palace Farmer's Market.(n)

programme.title: The Brecon Beacons with Iolo Williams
programme.desc: Part one of two. wildlife expert e explores the unique scenery of the Brecon Beacons over the course of a year, braving the elements in each season to detail the ever-changing landscape of the valleys. He begins in the winter enduring a raging blizzard on the high peaks, while the lowlands are bathed in sun. Later, nesting is in full swing as spring arrives and while hundreds of dotterel are resting in the Black Mountains during their journey from Africa to breeding sites in Scotland, peregrines are nesting in an old quarry in the Central Beacons.(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the fifth semi-final of the quiz, inviting four returning contenders to brave the black chair once more. They answer questions on the specialist subjects of the Trans-Siberian Railway, Dusty Springfield, the Monty Python films and Grayson Perry, before they each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge.(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as the competition reaches its climax after getting under way last July, when 28 teams entered the contest with high hopes. They were gradually whittled dow to the final two after a grand total of 36 first- and second-round matches, then the quarter- and semi-finals. The two teams will be endeavouring to succeed last year's winners the University of Edinburgh and become the 49th champions of the quiz, carrying off the trophy designed by Manchester sculptor Adrian Moakes.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: The Restaurant That Burns Off Calories
programme.desc: Maitre d' Fred Sirieix and doctor Zoe Williams open a restaurant with a difference, welcoming 20 unsuspecting diners for a slap-up meal. It all sounds normal enough, but at the back of house is a fully functioning gym, where a group of fitness fanatics are poised on exercise bikes, treadmills and rowing machines, ready to burn off every single calorie ordered and consumed by the diners. This experiment is based on scientific research that suggests that people eat 20 percent less when shown how much effort it takes to remove excess calories.(n)

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: According to one report, the three most stressful experiences in life are the death of a spouse, divorce, and imprisonment. Moving house is only 32nd on the list, but anything can happen in this tale involving the Handle Me Gently removals company and a nervous resident. Written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Emilia Fox, Monica Dolan and Nick Moran.(n)

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Highlights from the satirical news show, presented by Nish Kumar, as a cast of comedy correspondents give their take on the week's events.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Sicily: Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Part one of two. Michael Scott traces the history of Sicily, from the first arrival of ancient Greek settlers 3,000 years ago to the arrival of the Byzantine Empire. He begins his journey on the slopes of Mount Etna, Europe's largest active volcano, and discovers how the Greeks began to settle on Sicily's east coast - planting their olives and vines and building great city states that soon came to rival even Athens itself.(n)

programme.title: The Private Life of...
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty reveals the hidden lives of farmyard animals, beginning with an investigation into the nature of cows. Visiting Devon and Northumberland, he discovers how the hierarchy of a herd is established, tests their intelligence by trying to teach three animals how to demand food using a bell, and discovers what could turn a placid creature into a potential killer.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.sub-title: Coast Great Guides: Heart of England's South Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver and Tessa Dunlop present a guide to England's south coast from Brighton to Chesil, revealing what makes these two pebble beaches special. Neil also takes a trip on a flotilla of boats that includes a prototype yacht being prepared for the America's Cup, a vessel designed to help save the Solent oyster population and an offshore powerboat. Plus, a round-up of the best Coast stories from the past 10 years.(n)

programme.title: Twin
programme.desc: Ingrid picks up Karin from the police officer's house and finds her revelations could have major consequences, while Erik discovers Adam's dark secrets. Frank begins to investigate the circumstances that led to the accident and gets a startling confession. In Norwegian.(n)

programme.title: Twin
programme.desc: Erik confronts Ingrid over Adam's dark side and is surprised to discover that his brother's life was far from perfect. Karin struggles at school, but when Erik tries to play the role of a dedicated father, he only reinforces her suspicion that something is seriously wrong and she decides to take matters into her own hands. In Norwegian.(n)

programme.title: What a Performance! Pioneers of Popular Entertainment
programme.desc: The golden age of variety theatre, from the start of the 20th century to the outbreak of the Second World War, which starred the likes of George Formby and Gracie Fields.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Jackie Brambles host the edition first shown on July 13, featuring performances by Waterfront, Bette Midler, De La Soul, Danny Wilson, Michael Jackson, Kirsty MacColl and Gloria Estefan.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first shown on July 20, featuring performances by Sonia, London Boys, the Cult, Bobby Brown, Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers, Eartha Kitt and Bronski Beat, and Monie Love.(n)

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.sub-title: Soul & Beyond with Corinne Bailey Rae & Trevor Nelson
programme.desc: DJ Trevor Nelson and singer-songwriter Corinne Bailey Rae present their ultimate soundtrack in an hour of classic and contemporary soul and R'n'B gems. Given an airing are childhood favourites such as the Jackson 5 and Gladys Knight. as well as inspirational tracks from Prince, Mary J Blige, Stevie Wonder and Marvin Gaye. As they watch their selection, the duo reveal the reasons behind their choices.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.sub-title: Coast Great Guides: Heart of England's South Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver and Tessa Dunlop present a guide to England's south coast from Brighton to Chesil, revealing what makes these two pebble beaches special. Neil also takes a trip on a flotilla of boats that includes a prototype yacht being prepared for the America's Cup, a vessel designed to help save the Solent oyster population and an offshore powerboat. Plus, a round-up of the best Coast stories from the past 10 years.(n)

programme.title: The Private Life of...
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty reveals the hidden lives of farmyard animals, beginning with an investigation into the nature of cows. Visiting Devon and Northumberland, he discovers how the hierarchy of a herd is established, tests their intelligence by trying to teach three animals how to demand food using a bell, and discovers what could turn a placid creature into a potential killer.(n)

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the history of the fishing village of Port Isaac in Cornwall, discovering a place where people exploited a range of natural resources to make a living and find profits far beyond Britain's shores.(n)

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network. A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves to be problematic, and Robbie helps out a stranded boater.(n)

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the connections between the wildlife, landscape and people of the Arabian peninsula. The first edition features animals including oryxes, jerboas, horned vipers and scorpions, and a journey with the Bedouin - camel-riding nomads who are the only humans to have mastered the art of survival in the desert. Narrated by Alexander Siddig.(n)

programme.title: A Midsummer Night's Dream
programme.desc: A woman and her lover elope, pursued by her jealous suitor and his lovesick admirer. They all fall under the spell of a mischievous fairy, causing their feelings for each other to run wild. Elsewhere, a bumbling troupe of would-be thespians is having difficulty in performing their play when the leading man undergoes a strange transformation. Russell T Davies's adaptation of Shakespeare's comedy, starring Maxine Peake, Matt Lucas and John Hannah.(n)

programme.title: One-Hit Wonders at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of hits by artists that were hardly heard from again after one of their songs climbed to the pinnacle of pop, alongside an analysis of the 'one-hit wonder' phenomenon. Featuring Peter Sarstedt's 1969 track Where Do You Go To (My Lovely)?, Trio's 1982 smash Da Da Da, Move Closer, by Phyllis Nelson, and You Get What You Give, by New Radicals.(n)

programme.title: Jago: A Life Underwater
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of Rohani - an 80-year-old Indonesian hunter who hobbles around on land, but once he hits the water is able to dive like a fish on a single breath, descending to great depths for several minutes to stalk his prey like a true underwater predator.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Bolton
programme.desc: Oxford historian Dr Janina Ramirez sets reformed forger Shaun Greenhalgh his hardest task yet - to carve an Islamic bottle out of rock crystal in the style of the 10th-century Egyptian Fatimids. Rock crystal is notoriously fragile and sourcing the right quantities of it is almost impossible, but the real problems begin when the carving is finished.(n)

programme.title: Becoming a Lied Singer: Thomas Quasthoff and the Art of German Song
programme.desc: Drawing on his multiple roles as maestro, teacher and founder of the Das Lied International singing competition, Thomas Quasthoff takes audiences on a personal journey into this short, but intensely expressive art form. He focuses on Franz Schubert as the first great Lieder writer, and in Hamburg delves into the life and work of Johannes Brahms, a composer he feels a great empathy with. The programme includes rare archive footage of Thomas performing with pianist AndrÃ¡s Schiff in 2003 (prior to his retirement from the classical stage), as well as a newly restored telerecording of Thomas's Lied singing hero, Dietrich Fischer-Dieskau.(n)

programme.title: Utopia: In Search of the Dream
programme.desc: Art historian Professor Richard Clay explores how the idea of utopia has been re-invented by generations of writers and artists. He also examines what utopian visions reveal about our deepest hopes, dreams and fears. Among the contrributors is Star Trek actress Nichelle Nichols.(n)

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the connections between the wildlife, landscape and people of the Arabian peninsula. The first edition features animals including oryxes, jerboas, horned vipers and scorpions, and a journey with the Bedouin - camel-riding nomads who are the only humans to have mastered the art of survival in the desert. Narrated by Alexander Siddig.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as demonstrates his painting technique as he creates tranquil scenes taken from nature, beginning with mountains, evergreens, paths and bushes.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Maps
programme.sub-title: Medieval Maps - Mapping the Medieval Mind
programme.desc: Cartography through the ages, beginning with the Hereford Mappa Mundi, the largest intact medieval wall map in the world, which was intended to picture all of human knowledge in a single image. The programme traces its history, and features interviews with people who have been affected by it, including Turner Prize-winning artist Grayson Perry.(n)

programme.title: Meet the Romans with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The classicist takes a look at the history of the ancient city, putting aside the bloody stories of emperors and armies to learn about the ordinary Roman on the street. Her travels take her from the Via Appia roadway, originally built in 312 BC, to the Colosseum and Monte Testaccio, an artificial hill made of broken crockery. Along the way, she tells the stories of a slave-turned-baker who made a fortune out of the grain trade, building his tomb in the shape of a giant bread-oven, three prisoners of war and a purple-dye seller whose raw material was imported from Tunisia.(n)

programme.title: Da Vinci: The Lost Treasure
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce travels to Florence, Milan, Paris and Warsaw to tell the story of Leonardo da Vinci's life, exploring why a man whose reputation rests on only a few pictures - including the Mona Lisa - is regarded as one of the greatest painters of all time. She also visits New York to see a recently discovered piece by the artist.(n)

programme.title: Patrick Kielty's Mulholland Drive
programme.desc: Patrick Kielty's journey to find the truth about Belfast-born William Mulholland, who was responsible for building Los Angeles' water infrastructure and providing a water supply that allowed the city to grow into one of the largest in the world. Patrick's family all worked for the water company in Northern Ireland and upon discovering that the man who gave LA its water was from Belfast, decided to find out why he isn't globally recognised.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Outlaws: Highwaymen, Pirates and Rogues
programme.sub-title: Knights of the Road: The Highwayman's Story
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis explores the criminal underworld of Britain in the 17th and 18th centuries, beginning with the arrival of the highwayman in the wake of the English Civil War. Heavily romanticised in literature, these glamorous gangsters became a social menace on the roads and a political thorn in the side of the creaking British state, but a far darker reality lay underneath the dashing image of stylish robbers on horseback.(n)

programme.title: The Search for the Lost Manuscript: Julian of Norwich
programme.desc: Historian Dr Janina Ramirez tells the story of the first book written in English by a woman, Revelations of Divine Love by Julian of Norwich. The book was written in 1373 and presents an alternative viewpoint on man's relationship with God, which led to it being suppressed for more than 500 years. She travels from Norwich to Cambrai in Northern France, revealing how the book survived and the stories of women who championed it, and consults with academics and theologians about the book's importance.(n)

programme.desc: Funella has written the beginning to a new song to welcome guests into the Furchester Hotel. It's very very catchy. Cookie Monster tries singing him a new song, the Cookie Cookie Yum Yum song but that doesn't work either. Elmo suggests that he tries counting, which works for a little while, but then the song comes back into his head! But the Furchester's can solve any problem and when it seems that Harvey can't take it anymore; Funella announces that she has finished the welcome song! programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. Still feeling conflicted about his feelings, Dylan tries to avoid Faith as much as possible. Meanwhile, Rash goes against Connie's wishes when dealing with a challenging patient, and Ruby makes a life changing decision.(n)

programme.title: Outnumbered
programme.desc: Ben's school parents' evening takes an unexpected turn when he begins discussing muggers, dangerous chemicals and re-enactments of the Great Plague. Karen tries to decide which religion she would like to lead, while Sue still believes Jake is hiding something. Comedy, starring Daniel Roche and Ramona Marquez.(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: The couple announce their engagement, prompting the Shipmans to throw a party in Billericay. Even Bryn's decision to use his sat-nav for the journey fails to dampen their spirits, and everything goes swimmingly until the conversation turns to money - and the sweethearts drop a bombshell. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star.(n)

programme.title: All Round to Mrs Brown's
programme.desc: Mrs Brown's Boys creator, writer and star Brendan O'Carroll dons Agnes's famous cardigan and curly wig once again as he throws open the doors of the larger-than-life Irish matriarch's home to host the Saturday night chat show. In the fifth episode of the series, Agnes and his comical family are joined on the show by the comedian and The Masked Singer host Joel Dommett, and morning television presenters Ruth Langsford and Eamonn Holmes. Plus, British country duo The Shires perform live on the show.(n)

programme.desc: Dougie Vipond explores the impact of Covid-19 on farming, while Anne Lundon is the first of Landward's presenters to reveal her favourite stretch of coastline.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany berates Keegan for his bad mood and the chaos at the Taylors' only adds to his stress, leading to an angry outburst - but he is mortified to realise Bailey has witnessed his violent. Sonia urges Whitney to get back to normal, but that proves easier said than done with Michaela posting disagreeable online comments online, leaving her crushed. Ash cries on Vinny's shoulder but they both panic when Suki arrives home early, and after speaking to Callum, Stuart is inspired to propose to Rainie again.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Bake Off judge Prue Leith, reality TV star Scarlett Moffatt, rapper Big Narstie and weatherman Tomasz Schafernaker join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory.(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A student backpacker is found dead in the showers of her hostel, and with a gun in her hand and her body slumped against the only door, all evidence points toward suicide. Humphrey isn't so sure, however, especially when he notices all her toiletries laid out and her towel missing. But when the team question the victim's boyfriend and her fellow backpackers, they find themselves with very little to go on. Meanwhile, JP prepares for the biggest day of his life as his wedding day approaches, and when Humphrey bumps into an old lady friend, Florence encourages her boss to pursue his quest for love.(n)

programme.desc: Bake Off judge Prue Leith, reality TV star Scarlett Moffatt, rapper Big Narstie and weatherman Tomasz Schafernaker join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory.(n)

programme.title: The King's Hall of Fame
programme.desc: Comedian Jake O'Kane goes on a nostalgic trip back through the archives as he tells the story of the Belfast venue, which is currently being redeveloped into a wellbeing park. He reveals its place in the city's life since 1934, from the annual agricultural show to pop concerts, Ideal Home exhibitions to protest marches, and election counts to boxing.(n)

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: It may be a global pandemic but Covid 19 has hardly united the world around a collective response. We've seen world leaders focus on national self-interest rather than international collaboration. That could spell disaster for those countries least able to cope with a protracted public health crisis. Stephen Sackur speaks to David Miliband, President of the international charity the International Rescue Committee and a former UK Foreign Secretary. What kind of world will emerge from this pandemic?(n)

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The leader of the richest and most powerful nation on earth has just cut funding for the UN agency dedicated to the fight against global disease. What does this say about the ability of the international community to join forces to combat the threat of Covid-19? Stephen Sackur speaks to the former British Prime Minister Gordon Brown who is leading an effort to get G20 leaders to commit to a massive joint response to the pandemic. Is there any hope of real global cooperation in the current geopolitical climate?(n)

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: As the Coronavirus takes hold around the world, Global Questions invites its international audience to put their questions to a panel of experts on the risks to global health. Have lessons already been learned from the global response and how countries have handled the crisis in different ways. Join Zeinab Badawi and two leading international figures; the former Prime Minister of Denmark Helle Thorning-Schmidt and Richard Horton the Editor in Chief of the independent international Medical Journal the Lancet who will be answering your questions from around the world.(n)

Claudio then takes the chef to his family's citrus farm to discover local fruit the pompia, a citrus so bitter it is never eaten raw but makes a fabulous liqueur. Moving up to the hills, Ainsley cooks a traditional sheep stew with a group of shepherds, visits one of the island's ancient archaeological sites and cooks up a Sardinian-style minestrone.(n)

Lydia's determination to help Jyoti get better comes at a great personal cost to the doctor. Ted seeks closure as he prepares to leave India. Medical drama, starring Amanda Redman, James Krishna Floyd, Kenneth Cranham and Sayani Gupta.(n)

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: The conclusion of Julian Fellowes' drama about secrets and lies in uptight 19th-century London society. James travels to Manchester to investigate the allegations made against Charles, but soon realises there are greater threats at play. Lady Templemore employs Reggie to make Lady Maria change her mind, but her daughter will not give in so easily. Susan is in danger of losing everything after her secret is revealed, but as ever she uses her cunning to twist the situation to her own and others' benefit, while Charles is puzzled to receive a mysterious olive branch. Keen to end the feuding, he seeks out the offer of reconciliation - unaware he is being lured into danger.(n)

programme.title: Exposure
programme.desc: Jonathan Maitland presents an undercover report on the fast-growing sport of white-collar boxing, whereby people with little or no experience of fighting train to appear in special bouts, often for charity. Two undercover reporters attend training sessions at gyms in Leeds and London to find out how safe it is for those who take part.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp: On the NHS Frontline
programme.desc: First of two programmes in which Ross Kemp follows frontline medical staff in Milton Keynes Hospital Trust as they prepare for and face the coronavirus pandemic. He witnesses the sacrifices staff and their families are making in their battle against the disease. And he follows the journey of patients admitted into the hospital's respiratory ward with mild symptoms, through to the intensive care units, where those with severe Covid-19 fight for their lives.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: The team are in Hackney, where they surprise Jeroume and Onessa Cariazo, the parents of wheelchair-bound nine-year-old Elijah, who has rare genetic condition myotubular myopathy. Alan Titchmarsh, Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney give Elijah the sensory stimulation he craves, transforming the family's uneven overgrown urban courtyard into an accessible paradise complete with tropical water feature, a sensory beach and Asian-style dining zone.(n)

programme.title: A Mother's Son
programme.desc: First of a two-part drama, starring Hermione Norris, Martin Clunes, Paul McGann and Alexander Arnold. The murder of a schoolgirl rocks the residents of a quiet coastal town in Suffolk, and when Rosie stumbles upon a pair of bloodstained trainers hidden in her home, she suspects someone in her family may have been involved in the killing. With her son Jamie and stepson Rob both acting suspiciously, could she be right?(n)

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Summer of Love (San Francisco and Napa Valley)
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay, Gino D'Acampo and Fred Sirieix head to San Francisco, the free-spirited capital of the United States. They start out by sampling the latest trends of using cannabis in cooking, which was legalised in California in 2018 - but as they enjoy a hippy afternoon tea, Gino overindulges and chaos ensues, especially when the next activity on the agenda is a session of goat yoga. Moving on, the trio dig for clams on the Pacific coast, take a hot-air ride to a vineyard and visit a factory in Chinatown where fortune cookies are made. It seems a competition is in order and so the travel companions see who can make the most cookies in 60 seconds.(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott's tour of Sardinia continues in the Nuoro region, where his guide Claudio takes him to meet his parents, who run a small bakery specialising in pane carasau, a thin crispy bread found all over the island. Claudio then takes the chef to his family's citrus farm to discover local fruit the pompia, a citrus so bitter it is never eaten raw but makes a fabulous liqueur. Moving up to the hills, Ainsley cooks a traditional sheep stew with a group of shepherds, visits one of the island's ancient archaeological sites and cooks up a Sardinian-style minestrone.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team heads to the Yorkshire Dales to surprise Jim Thompson, a terminally ill 65-year-old grandfather and fundraiser. Despite Jim's own diagnosis, his commitment to spreading awareness and raising money for prostate cancer charities are legendary in the area, including creating a 'Calendar Girls' style naked calendar. Inspired by his bravery and dedication, the makeover team decide to turn Jim's overgrown and dangerous plot into a classic cottage garden with a Yorkshire twist.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

They start out by sampling the latest trends of using cannabis in cooking, which was legalised in California in 2018 - but as they enjoy a hippy afternoon tea, Gino overindulges and chaos ensues, especially when the next activity on the agenda is a session of goat yoga. Moving on, the trio dig for clams on the Pacific coast, take a hot-air ride to a vineyard and visit a factory in Chinatown where fortune cookies are made. It seems a competition is in order and so the travel companions see who can make the most cookies in 60 seconds.(n)

programme.desc: The first of two programmes celebrating the career of the Manchester-born comedy star. Les's youngest daughter Charlotte Dawson meets Opportunity Knocks director Royston Mayoh to hear how an audition would change her father's life for ever, Les's comedy partner Roy Barraclough reminisces about their famous characters Cissie and Ada, and Barry Cryer returns to Yorkshire Television to revisit some of Les's friends.(n)

programme.title: Les Dawson Forever
programme.desc: Part two of two. Members of the dance troupe the Roly Polys reunite on stage to reminisce about working alongside Les and Les's wife Tracy Dawson reunites with karaoke superfan Penny Morgan, who was given the opportunity to perform the duet of a lifetime on Cilla Black's Surprise Surprise in 1993.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran.(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The television presenter and comedian takes on an epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter, a spectacular but deadly time of year to see the unforgiving, ice-bound region. His odyssey is full of surprises as he witnesses natural wonders and discovers the magnetic lure of the far north. The first edition sees Alexander begin in Scandinavia and head toward Iceland, as he swims with locals in the ocean, faces the worst storm in 25 years, and goes down the world's first major tunnel to be built into the heart of a glacier.(n)

programme.title: The Great St Trinian's Train Robbery (1966)
programme.desc: A crook and his cronies stash the money from a train heist in a deserted old mansion, planning to recover it in good time. However, several years later, when the thieves arrive to collect their loot, they discover the building has been turned into a school for girls where the pupils have become infamous for their escapades. Comedy, starring Frankie Howerd, Dora Bryan, Reg Varney and George Cole.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Rosie and Max finally get married, but when she is injured by an elephant, a doctor has to give the newlyweds terrible news. Du Plessis is devastated when Caroline decides to stay in England - and Georgina makes enemies when she reveals she encouraged her sister not to return. Fatani declares his love to Vanessa, but realises she has feelings for Danny, while Alice has a risky solution for saving the elephant calf.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lydia is looking forward to a civilised hen do, but it is clear Mandy has more in store, and the stag party is under way, but a shock is in store when a hunky masseur arrives. Later, on their way to their night out, Mandy and Lydia are left stranded by their taxi driver on a country road, and Mandy manages to lose Lydia.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy feels total dread when Vinny's father reveals he has come back for his son, while Leyla and Kerry worry when they realise Lydia has yet to be seen on her wedding day. Dan insists to an attentive Amelia that he can look after himself, and Vanessa and Charity are dropped off at the chemotherapy unit.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy convinces a sad Paul that Vinny no longer lives with her, but, as everyone else enjoys the wedding reception, she is clearly troubled by the secret she's still keeping. Belle is taken aback when Jamie declares that he loves her and is going to leave Andrea for her.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: It's the day of Kevin and Maggie's wedding - but they're nowhere to be seen. As the members of the congregation fidget, Clive makes an accusation against Roger. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Josie Lawrence.(n)
