programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma and Audrey spend a day shopping in Southport. Samir and Tracy have a counselling session with the consultant. Steve invites Fiona on a business trip to Ireland.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily thanks Samir for being selfless in giving Tracy his kidney. Ken is stunned when Denise tells him she wants to start afresh.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff is worried that he failed to impress Linda. Betty takes advantage of Eric's emotional state. Kim wants to know what Dave sees in Kathy.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nellie fusses over Sam on his first proper day of work. Dave has a surprise in store for Kathy. Nellie and Zak's romantic meal out descends into chaos.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol asks Joe for assistance when suspicions mount against her following the death of a pensioner who she frequently visited. Peggy is annoyed to find a pile of rubbish has been dumped in her backyard and Gina shares her concerns with Carol as she approaches the end of her pregnancy.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As Brent Park Cricket Club prepare for the opening ceremony of their new pitch and pavilion, Mim arranges a big surprise for Rog - but disaster is waiting in the wings. Comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger gets a frosty response when he introduces new team member Clive to the others, while Kev and Mag present Mim with a motorised lawn-mower. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.sub-title: The Last of the Big Spenders
programme.desc: Rigsby invests in some new furniture in a desperate attempt to impress Ruth's replacement, Brenda, but makes the mistake of opting to pay for it on the never-never. Leonard Rossiter and Gay Rose star.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: After receiving a chilling phone call, the detective and sidekick DC Presley stumble on an exotic animal smuggling ring at a farm - where it appears an old adversary of Frost has been eaten by a crocodile. Meanwhile, a couple return from holiday to find the naked corpse of a man in their bedroom, prompting the husband to assume his wife was having an affair. Starring David Jason.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Two new residents put PC Mason in an awkward position both professionally and personally and, while the village rallies round to celebrate Blaketon's birthday, he gets more than he expected when Gina goes into labour. David decides to learn to play the piano. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, Yorkshire. Starring Joe McFadden, Tricia Penrose and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dave and Kathy tell Betty about their engagement. Nellie and Zak aren't on speaking terms, and Linda makes a shock announcement.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy is devastated about what she knows about Dave and Kim. Danny doesn't believe he is the father of Linda's child, and Terry's happy hour doesn't take off at the Woolpack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel picks on Tricia for always being late to work. Samir suggests to Deirdre that they should stay in Weatherfield after the operation, and Reg forces Maureen into work.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bet warns Steve not to involve Vicky in any dodgy deals. Deirdre warms to the idea of staying in Weatherfield, and Reg takes Tricia on at the shop.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Two new residents put PC Mason in an awkward position both professionally and personally and, while the village rallies round to celebrate Blaketon's birthday, he gets more than he expected when Gina goes into labour. David decides to learn to play the piano. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, Yorkshire. Starring Joe McFadden, Tricia Penrose and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: In the summer of 1967, Morse and Thursday are both devastated by Joan's abrupt departure from Oxford. The young detective responds to his loss by throwing himself into his work, investigating the suspicious drowning of a scientist who was part of a research team working on an early computer. At first it appears to be a simple suicide - until two more bodies are found in the river.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Two-part drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and based on the novel by Peter Robinson. DCI Alan Banks investigates the most difficult case of his life when five girls go missing but only four bodies are found. Chief suspect Marcus Payne is in a coma, and ambitious DS Annie Cabbot from the Professional Standards department pursues Banks in an effort to find out why. Andrea Lowe and Charlotte Riley also star.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Conclusion. The mysterious death of serial killer Marcus Payne makes the task of finding his accomplice more difficult and holds up the search for Leanne Wray. With time against him and DS Annie Cabbot breathing down his neck, Banks follows a hunch and takes Payne's wife in for questioning on suspicion of involvement with the rape and murder of the four girls. The lead she provides is more startling than anything the detectives are prepared for. Drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Charlotte Riley and Andrea Lowe.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Rog gets busy organising the team's first match, while Mim tries to cheer up Bob after Ginnie gives him his marching orders. Maggie's sister inadvertently lets slip a secret - with devastating consequences. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Kevin and Maggie have an awkward conversation about their fertility problems, while Rog and Mim arrive to discuss the forthcoming marriage blessing. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Dr Gaddas tells Steve he should take Oliver to A&E as a precaution, but a panicky Leanne arrives at the hospital, and is horrified to discover Steve left Oliver with Summer. Gemma finally accepts help for her post-natal depression, Ken's campaign gets off to an unfortunate start and Kelly encourages Asha to get back with Corey.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Amy's party soon gets out of hand when naked pictures of Asha are sent from Corey's phone to the â€˜party people' group chat and Corey accidentally smashes one of Tracy's new lamps. Leanne catches Steve red-handed, Johnny has an unwanted visitor and Ed and Aggie sit down to a romantic dinner.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Asha is sickened to realise somebody posted her naked pictures and they have gone viral, while Leanne is furious to realise Steve is seriously considering taking legal advice. Johnny takes matters into his own hands and Ken's election campaign is on the up.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is approached by a former mentor from his college days, who asks Morse to ensure the safety of his wife. However, the assignment leaves him contemplating his future with the police force. Meanwhile, a murder in the world of fashion stirs up old gangland conflicts in the city. Detective drama series, starring Shaun Evans as the young Inspector Morse.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Up the Khyber (1968)
programme.desc: British forces in colonial India find themselves in deep trouble when a manic local ruler discovers the soldiers' fearsome reputation is thoroughly undeserved - prompting open rebellion. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Roy Castle, Joan Sims, Bernard Bresslaw and Terry Scott.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A comic convention is held in Carver Valley, but the event gets off to a sinister start when the latest creation of a mysterious writer is stolen from the organiser's house and burned. Barnaby takes up the case, which turns murderous when a former supermodel is killed during the event. The only clue is an unusual issue of the missing comics, which seems to reveal the dark secrets of six local people - and provides the police with six likely suspects. Guest starring Bill Bailey and Jemma Redgrave.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.sub-title: ITV3 Most Wanted - Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The 'Most Wanted' episode of Agatha Christie's Marple, as voted by the public. Which classic of the genre will it be?(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death by a giant box during a magic show organised by famous illusionist Gideon Latimer, pathologist Kate discovers the cables had been deliberately sabotaged. Evangelising curate Andrew Maplin claims the tragedy is God's vengeance for the pagan traditions still held deep in Midsomer Oaks, but Barnaby and Nelson fear Gideon was the real target. Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee and Tamzin Malleson star in the murder mystery, with Joe Absolom, Andrew-Lee Potts, Justin Salinger, Amanda Burton and Stephanie Leonidas.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Girls (1973)
programme.desc: The council of a seaside town is in dire need of a means to attract tourists to its resort, so announces a beauty contest. While the conniving organiser plots a series of publicity stunts, an outraged feminist group plans to sabotage the event. Comedy, starring Sid James, June Whitfield, Joan Sims, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw and Peter Butterworth.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran.(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Steve from Blackpool searches for his birth mother who gave him up for adoption when he was a baby. After her partner tragically died when she was only 17-years-old, she had no choice but to try and find a new family for her son, and put an advert in the local paper to try and find him the best possible parents. Meanwhile, Corine searches for the son she gave up for adoption when she was just 15-years-old, but fears he may not forgive her once he finds out she has since adopted two other children.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger insists on leading the team convoy of cars to a fixture in the country but his map-reading skills let him down en route, and a run-in with a troublesome tractor driver ensures the day ends dismally. Richard Harris's comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Josie Lawrence and Timothy Spall.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Mim tries to persuade Roger to cancel a match with Piggy Pearson's team because of an incident with the latter's divorced sister. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Clapham Cook
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When a spate of petty thefts culminates in a series of murders at a student hostel, Miss Lemon persuades the Belgian sleuth to investigate. As ever, he receives support from Chief Inspector Japp. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis and Paris Jefferson.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boxer John Kinsella's successful world title bid stirs up hidden passions among the residents of Midsomer Morchard. As plans to stage a celebratory re-enactment of a Victorian prizefight shape up, jealousy, betrayal and blackmail take a ringside seat, while two suspicious deaths suggest Barnaby has let friendship cloud his judgement. Detective drama, guest starring Phil Daniels (Quadrophenia, EastEnders), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A planning dispute leads to murder after a stalwart in the Midsomer Conservation Society suspects her neighbours are involved in a money-making development scam. Political wrangling, burglaries and sexual liaisons abound in picturesque Great Pelfe as Barnaby and Jones investigate. Guest starring Hugo Speer (Bleak House), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor.(n)

David decides to learn to play the piano. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, Yorkshire. Starring Joe McFadden, Tricia Penrose and Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: In the summer of 1967, Morse and Thursday are both devastated by Joan's abrupt departure from Oxford. The young detective responds to his loss by throwing himself into his work, investigating the suspicious drowning of a scientist who was part of a research team working on an early computer. At first it appears to be a simple suicide - until two more bodies are found in the river.(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Two-part drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and based on the novel by Peter Robinson. DCI Alan Banks investigates the most difficult case of his life when five girls go missing but only four bodies are found. Chief suspect Marcus Payne is in a coma, and ambitious DS Annie Cabbot from the Professional Standards department pursues Banks in an effort to find out why. Andrea Lowe and Charlotte Riley also star.(n)

programme.title: World of Sport: The Good, the Bad & the Ugly
programme.desc: Richard Henwood delves into the ITV sport archives to take a look back at the careers of racing drivers James Hunt and Barry Sheene, and a spectacular crash by Kelvin Burt.(n)

programme.title: Tommy Cooper
programme.desc: The incomparable comedian performs a variety of sketches, with the assistance of guest Bernard Cribbins.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar investigates when a trusted diplomat friend goes missing with $1million, while his daughter is adducted. Robert Hardy guest stars, with Roger Moore.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is assigned to protect an Middle Eastern diplomat who is being pursued by assassins. Bodie and Doyle find themselves relentlessly pursued by a gang of assailants that seems able to accurately anticipate their every move, leading to a tense car chase and a riverside shoot-out. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's holiday is disrupted by the escape of a violent psychopath from prison. Tim Cook, dubbed 'The Leopard' by the press has vowed murderous revenge on the detective inspector for his earlier capture, and has friends on the outside prepared to harbour him while he plans his attack. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.sub-title: A Number of Old Wives' Tales
programme.desc: Apparently successful entrepreneur Clive Cosgrove loses his 'confidence' after Arthur and Terry attend his wedding celebrations. Vintage comedy drama, guest starring Patrick Mower, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur witnesses the untimely death of a business acquaintance, for which Chisholm appears to hold an old friend of Terry's accountable. In the circumstances, a spell in hospital seems like a good idea - but not all of Arthur's visitors arrive with flowers and grapes. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: As the winter season enters its final few weeks, the price of lobsters rises above 100 dollars per kilo, and a bunch of new boats try to capitalise on the high price. Meanwhile the Triton is hunting for big reds near Low Rocky Point, but other boats aren't the only problem - octopus are out in force, preying on rock lobster.(n)

programme.title: The Sting (1973)
programme.desc: Two conmen decide to swindle a mobster out of a fortune in revenge for the murder of a mutual friend. Enlisting the help of a host of crooks, they quickly conjure up a brilliant scheme to make him pay for his crime. Oscar-winning Depression-era comedy drama, starring Robert Redford, Paul Newman, Robert Shaw, Charles Durning and Eileen Brennan.(n)

programme.title: Red River (1948)
programme.desc: Conflict grows between a tough Texan rancher and his Civil War veteran foster son during a 1,000 mile cattle drive to Missouri. The older man's uncompromising methods and unyielding outlook lead his workers to mutiny - and the son ends up siding with the rebellious ranch hands against his father. Howard Hawks' Western, starring John Wayne, Montgomery Clift, Walter Brennan, Joanne Dru and Coleen Gray.(n)

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A Â£30,000 consignment of mercury is stolen and the squad is bemused when the prime suspect comes up with a cast-iron alibi - he was in the company of his 'mistress' hours before his planned wedding ceremony. Diana Dors guest stars in the crime drama, alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter track down those responsible for killing a driver and injuring a policeman while making off with vehicles carrying brandy on two separate occasions. Carter's new girlfriend, rookie cop Julie Kingdom, pursues a known fence and recovers the brandy. However, when Regan wants to take the credit, he and Carter come to blows. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: The Towering Inferno (1974)
programme.desc: The world's tallest building goes up in flames during the opening celebrations. While the architect who designed the skyscraper tries to save the party-goers trapped on the 135th floor, a fire chief leads a valiant attempt to fight the blaze. Disaster movie, starring Paul Newman, Steve McQueen, Faye Dunaway, William Holden, Fred Astaire, Richard Chamberlain, Susan Blakely and Robert Vaughn.(n)

programme.title: Fargo (1996)

Comedy thriller from the Coen brothers, starring Frances McDormand, Steve Buscemi and William H Macy.(n)

Robbie Coltrane and Art Malik guest star, with George Cole and Patrick Malahide.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A newly appointed coroner faces a baptism of fire as local community figures call for his resignation almost as soon as he takes up the post.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo receives death threats from an ex-convict with a vendetta - but he is not the only one in danger, as the vengeful crook targets the detective's niece on her wedding day.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar finds a suspicious building in Wales which is being used as a research laboratory, plunging him into the midst of a spine-chilling adventure. Spy drama, directed by and starring Roger Moore.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.sub-title: A Number of Old Wives' Tales
programme.desc: Apparently successful entrepreneur Clive Cosgrove loses his 'confidence' after Arthur and Terry attend his wedding celebrations. Vintage comedy drama, guest starring Patrick Mower, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is faced with a terrifying campaign of explosions and assassinations, apparently committed by an opposing operative that Cowley is convinced he killed several years earlier. It appears that he is either mistaken or an old associate of his former adversary is seeking revenge. Gordon Jackson stars.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A bad dream has Regan's girlfriend fearing for his safety, and her visions ring true when he gets involved in a case involving a stolen lorryload of cigars. The plot thickens when illicit cocaine is retrieved and arms stolen from the US are discovered to be on their way to a military faction in Ireland. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner is forced to set aside his scalpel and rush to an Arizona Indian reservation to help his foster son Chester, who is suffering from bubonic plague.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo's niece is kidnapped on her birthday by criminals who, demand the release of their accomplice, who is in custody for killing a police officer. Guest starring Richard Gere.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar investigates the suspicious deaths of five company directors, who each received a card with the words 'The Scales of Justice' printed on it prior to their demise. However, he finds his inquiries frustrated by the remaining members of the board. Roger Moore stars.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry is assigned to mind a writer of romantic fiction with a drink problem, whose ex-husband has sold their house behind her back. Beryl Reid and Bill Maynard guest star, alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are pitted against KGB assassins when a CI5 operative is apparently raised from the dead. A proposed exchange finds Cowley at a deserted aerodrome, and a burst of gunfire apparently leaves his life on the line. Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson star.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A former criminal and friend of Regan's wins a large amount of money on the pools - but then receives blackmailing phone calls regarding a crime from 11 years earlier for which he was never caught. Hard-hitting drama, guest starring Edward Judd, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes at British brands, including creating the soles of Dr Marten's, and making Wagon Wheels. Narrated by Jimmy Nail.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.sub-title: Killer from the Abyss - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jeremy Wade takes on the biggest investigation of his career - to unravel what happened to the passengers of the RMS Laconia in the Atlantic.(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: Despite his reputation for finding big-money hauls, skipper Peter finds pickings slim at the end of the winter season. Elsewhere, the Minnamurra is heading back to port as Clive plans to motor through the night to try and capitalise on the current high lobster price.(n)

programme.title: Rocky (1976)

programme.title: Channel 4 News

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.sub-title: Cruising South America with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Jane sails around South America in style aboard an ultra-luxury ship. Starting in ValparaÃ­so, she embraces her inner Banksy on a street art tour, before beginning her journey south along the Chilean coast. A few days later she reaches the breathtaking glacier on Laguna San Rafael. Here, Jane partakes in the customary and traditional drinking of whisky on ice directly from the glacier. She then ventures further south into deepest Patagonia, stopping off in Punta Arenas. Back on board the ship, Jane enjoys a tour of the bridge with the captain as they make their way to the Falklands.(n)

programme.title: Philip: The King Without a Crown
programme.desc: A profile of the Duke of Edinburgh, exploring how he dealt with giving up his promising naval career and his surname as he took a back seat to the Queen. From his charity patronages, to founding the Duke of Edinburgh award and becoming an unlikely champion of environmental issues, his life as Britain's longest-serving royal consort has been a significant one for the entire nation.(n)

programme.title: The Royal Family: Affairs & Infidelities

programme.title: Cher: The Highs, the Lows & All the Hits
programme.desc: Intercut with dramatised extracts, this documentary relives the meteoric highs and devastating lows ofÂ the singer and actress's career in showbusiness. It lifts the lid on the crucial events and key players in her life - from abandonmentÂ by a drug-addict father and her time in an orphanage as a baby, to her mother's frustrated acting career with a revolving door of husbands. Cher's relationship with SonnyÂ Bono is also explored - a longed-for father figure whose controlling ways almost broke her.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: In one of the biggest engineering projects of the Middle Ages, Salisbury Cathedral was moved from a wind-blasted Iron Age hill fort to its current location - a feat accomplished in just 38 years, helping establish the Wiltshire city in the process. A place of both ancient tradition and home to one of the country's oldest choirs, Tony witnesses the ritual of inducting a new chorister and sees the oldest working clock in the world. The presenter also reflects on the cathedral's role in a clandestine operation that helped turn the tide of the Second World War.(n)

programme.desc: Feature-length film taking viewers on a journey aboard a Stanier Class 5 4-6-0 locomotive from the foothills of Ben Nevis down the West Highland route to meet the sea at the port of Mallaig. The programme is with train driver Ian Riley and train guard and manager Florence MacClean for the length of the route as the steam train winds its way past lochs, mountains and the world-famous Glenfinnan viaduct, all the while taking in the beautiful ancient vistas of indigenous ferns, heather and bracken.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor and presenter visits Durham cathedral, one of Britain's first Unesco World Heritage sites and a leading tourist attraction. The cathedral is over 900 years old and built like a fortress. It is 218ft tall and when first constructed was the country's tallest building. The bishops at Durham were so powerful that they were given the title Prince-Bishop to reflect their huge wealth and influence. As well as exploring the Norman edifice, Tony meets a modern-day treasure hunter who has discovered hidden lost treasures from the cathedral in the River Wear.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go Barging
programme.desc: Actor Tom Conti, mercurial gardener Diarmuid Gavin, ex-athlete Tessa Sanderson, singer Tony Christie and presenter Penny Smith explore and enjoy life in the slow lane aboard two barges, narrow-boating along two of France's best-loved waterways - the River Lot and the Canal du Midi. After meeting each other and moving into their boats, the usually unflappable Penny needs rescuing after tangling with a tree. Tom takes charge of their mini-fleet, but gets into a scrape of his own, while below deck Diarmuid targets Tony for some gentle ribbing, while Tessa resents being bossed about.(n)

programme.title: Around the World by Train with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor sets off from Seattle on an Amtrak Cascades train into Canada, where he is struck by the beauty of Vancouver and the warmth of the locals. He then takes a two-day rail journey aboard the Rocky Mountaineer. While the other passengers relax and enjoy the luxury service, Tony is put to work in its kitchen. Lastly, he travels via Alaska to the small town of Talkeetna. Boarding a float plane, Tony flies over snow-covered mountains to reach the base of the Alaskan glaciers, where he witnesses the impact of climate change up close.(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A car thief in Wakefield rams his way out of a petrol station before leading Jaffa, Chris and the rest of the team on a high-speed and highly dangerous pursuit. Big H and Stef can't believe their luck when two drug dealers park up right in front their unmarked car, while Wayne and Tom face some fighting talk from a 17-year-old, and a late-night collision with a Mini highlights the risks officers face on the road.(n)

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: At Ripley police station, officer Alex responds to news of two cars reported missing by a young woman, and the suspected thieves turn out to be two wanted men with a long list of previous convictions. Alex makes it his mission to catch the two dangerous criminals, who go on a violent rampage while on the run for four weeks. Jamie Theakston presents.(n)

With Jason Statham and Jet Li.(n)

programme.desc: A leaky boat leaves the precocious youngsters stranded on a jungle island, but their luck changes when explorers the Wild Thornberrys come to their rescue. However, Thornberry dad Nigel loses his memory, so it falls to his daughter Eliza to save the day. Children's animated adventure, with the voices of Nancy Cartwright, Bruce Willis, Lacey Chabert, Elizabeth Daily and Tim Curry.(n)

programme.title: Hotel for Dogs (2009)
programme.desc: An orphaned brother and sister face losing their beloved dog when they go into a foster home that does not allow pets. The kids find the ideal hiding place in an abandoned hotel, and set about transforming it into luxury canine accommodation for not just their beloved pet, but every stray in town. Family comedy, starring Emma Roberts, Jake T Austin, Don Cheadle and Lisa Kudrow.(n)

programme.title: Shrek the Third (2007)
programme.desc: The bad-tempered ogre finds himself in the unwelcome position of being next in line for the throne. Preferring to stay at home in the swamps, he sets out on a quest to find the rightful heir - a rebellious teenager named Artie - and persuade him to face up to his responsibilities as the future king. Meanwhile, the ambitious Prince Charming sees his chance to take the throne. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Justin Timberlake, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas and Rupert Everett.(n)

programme.title: The Rewrite (2014)
programme.desc: A washed-up Hollywood screenwriter takes a job at a small university, planning to exploit his position as a local celebrity and do as little work as possible. His cynical outlook begins to change when he falls for a single mother working toward her degree, while a talented pupil makes him consider what he wants from his life. Romantic comedy, with Hugh Grant, Marisa Tomei, Allison Janney and JK Simmons.(n)

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen (2003)
programme.desc: Fearless hunter Allan Quatermain teams up with a host of other characters from Victorian literature - including Captain Nemo, the Invisible Man, Tom Sawyer and Dr Jekyll - to put a stop to the nefarious Fantom's evil plot to bring about a world war. Fantasy adventure, based on Alan Moore's comic-book series, starring Sean Connery, Shane West, Stuart Townsend, Richard Roxburgh, Peta Wilson and Tony Curran.(n)

programme.title: Assassin's Creed (2016)
programme.desc: A convict facing execution is recruited for a secret experiment where his consciousness is sent back in time to experience the life of his ancestor - a 15th-century ancestor battling a secret society. With the same evil organisation plotting world domination in the present, he must master his predecessor's skills and unlock his secret knowledge to defeat them. Action adventure based on the video game series, starring Michael Fassbender, Marion Cotillard, Jeremy Irons and Brendan Gleeson.(n)

programme.title: Blair Witch (2016)
programme.desc: Twenty years have passed since the victims of the Blair Witch disappeared in the woods in Maryland, with James still wondering what happened to his sister Heather. When he discovers eerie internet footage of someone resembling her, he and three friends set out to investigate, taking along cameras to document their search. Horror sequel, starring James Allen McCune, Callie Hernandez, Brandon Scott, Valorie Curry, Corbin Reid and Wes Robinson.(n)

programme.title: Thelma (2017)
programme.desc: A student starts to experience extreme seizures while studying at university in Oslo and soon learns that the violent episodes are a symptom of inexplicable - and often dangerous - supernatural abilities. Thriller, starring Eili Harboe and Kaya Wilkins. In Norwegian.(n)

programme.title: Edward Scissorhands (1990)
programme.desc: A reclusive genius creates a boy but dies before completing him, leaving his brainchild with scissor-like blades in place of hands. A kindly woman from a nearby suburb takes pity on the lonely figure and welcomes him into her home. He quickly becomes a hit in the neighbourhood - but then his new friends start to turn against him. Tim Burton's fantasy, starring Johnny Depp, Winona Ryder, Dianne Wiest and Vincent Price.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Teacher (1959)
programme.desc: A headmaster hopes to land a more prestigious job at a better school, but his pupils do not want him to leave. When two inspectors pay a visit, the mischievous students see a chance to sabotage his career plans with a series of pranks. Comedy, starring Ted Ray, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Kenneth Williams.(n)

programme.title: Ice Age: The Meltdown (2006)
programme.desc: Sid the sloth, Manny the mammoth and Diego the sabre-toothed tiger grimly realise the good life is about to come to an abrupt end as flood waters threaten their homes. However, a vulture suggests they could turn an old giant tree into a boat and make their escape, so the three friends set off in search of it. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Ray Romano, John Leguizamo and Denis Leary.(n)

programme.title: Home Alone (1990)
programme.desc: A boy has to fend for himself when he is accidentally left behind after his family jets off to Paris for Christmas. The youngster initially enjoys his freedom, but is soon forced to resort to ingenious booby traps to defend the family home from two dim-witted burglars. Comedy, starring Macaulay Culkin, Joe Pesci, Daniel Stern, John Heard, Catherine O'Hara and John Candy.(n)

programme.title: Home Again (2017)
programme.desc: Five months after separating from her husband, Alice Kinney and her daughters relocate from New York to her childhood home in Los Angeles, which belonged to her late father, Oscar-winning film director John Kinney. Eventually things take an unexpected turn when she lets three young guys move in with her. Romantic comedy, starring Reese Witherspoon, Michael Sheen and Candice Bergen.(n)

programme.title: Snatched (2017)
programme.desc: After being dumped by her boyfriend before their holiday, Emily Middleton takes her cautious mother Linda with her to Ecuador. However, their lives are soon turned upside down when the pair are kidnapped. Fish-out-of-water comedy, starring Amy Schumer, Goldie Hawn and Joan Cusack.(n)

programme.title: Pyewacket (2017)
programme.desc: An angst-ridden teenage girl awakens something in the woods when she naively performs an occult ritual to kill her mother. Horror, starring Nicole Munoz, Laurie Holden and Chloe Rose.(n)

programme.title: In Fear (2013)
programme.desc: A couple go to Ireland for a music festival, but get hopelessly lost when driving through a series of country roads. Their plight turns deadly as they are terrorised by mysterious figures lurking in the darkness, while an encounter with a seemingly harmless local brings dangers of its own. Horror, starring Iain De Caestecker, Alice Englert and Allen Leech.(n)

programme.title: The Visit (2015)
programme.desc: Two children are sent by their parents to spend a week at their grandparents' remote farm. They are confused by the old couple's strange house rules, but as they investigate, they discover something disturbing is going on - and that they may not be returning home alive. Horror, starring Olivia DeJonge and Ed Oxenbould.(n)

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: Comedy about a family of deaf gangsters who protect their interests with their signing hands. When the opera family threaten their dominance a fingerspelling war ensues.(n)

programme.title: Time Bandits (1981)
programme.desc: A bemused schoolboy is escorted on a bizarre journey through time by a group of larcenous dwarfs. His adventures lead him to encounters with various characters from history - but none of them are quite what he had imagined. Terry Gilliam's fantasy comedy, starring Craig Warnock, David Rappaport, John Cleese, Michael Palin, Sean Connery, Ian Holm, Ralph Richardson and David Warner.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Cabby (1963)
programme.desc: The neglected wife of a workaholic taxi owner grows increasingly tired of his lack of interest in their marriage. In a ploy to bring him to his senses, she secretly launches a rival cab firm with a driver roster made up of attractive women, who soon tempt his customers away. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Connor and Charles Hawtrey.(n)

programme.title: Hulk (2003)
programme.desc: Scientist Dr Bruce Banner turns into the rampaging green Hulk after exposure to a huge dose of gamma radiation, prompting the military to embark on a no-holds-barred mission to destroy the monster. Meanwhile, Banner's unstable father resurfaces, holding the secret to his transformation. Comic-book adventure from director Ang Lee, starring Eric Bana, Jennifer Connelly, Sam Elliott and Nick Nolte.(n)

programme.title: Exodus: Gods and Kings (2014)
programme.desc: Moses, the adoptive brother of the pharaoh of Egypt, is sent into exile, where he receives a message from God demanding he set all the country's slaves free. When his brother refuses to comply, the kingdom is struck by a series of devastating plagues. Biblical epic, starring Christian Bale, Joel Edgerton, Sigourney Weaver and John Turturro.(n)

programme.title: The Last Stand (2013)
programme.desc: Disgraced narcotics cop Ray Owens retires into a quiet life as sheriff of a small town on the Mexican border. He gets the chance to prove his worth as a law enforcer when a drug lord escapes from prison and tries to flee the US - with his escape route taking him through Ray's jurisdiction. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Johnny Knoxville and Forest Whitaker.(n)

programme.title: T2 Trainspotting (2017)
programme.desc: Recovering heroin addict Mark Renton returns to his Edinburgh roots after 20 years living overseas, hoping to make peace with the friends he abandoned. However, starting anew proves far from easy, especially when old partner in crime Begbie escapes from prison, still brooding on Renton's betrayal. Danny Boyle's drama sequel, starring Ewan McGregor, Robert Carlyle, Ewen Bremner and Jonny Lee Miller.(n)

programme.desc: Raj is asked to create a message on the off-chance a Nasa mission discovers alien life, and the guys fight over what he should do. Meanwhile, Penny contemplates returning to acting. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons, Kaley Cuoco and Kunal Nayyar.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam and Andi have a disagreement at the office and must prevent it from becoming a problem at home, whilethe kids try to extend Lowell's stay after realising he is an amazing cook.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Astronautical engineer Adam and junior doctor Steph kick the week of soirees in and around Berkshire. Their guests are all slightly bemused by Steph's 'fake laugh', but quickly work out that she's the boss of the relationship. The next hosts, stockbroker Simon and Liz, go for a gastropub-style menu, but not all of their guests are fans of their ingredients. On the final night, everyone is impressed by NHS workers Mat and Lee's menu, though behind the scenes there's plenty of drama.(n)

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: Liv throws a house party for the gang, but the fun soon turns sour for Zara when she encounters Sam's ex. Miles is delighted he and his new love interest are growing closer.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity First Dates
programme.desc: Four more celebrities go in search of romance with members of the public. Brad Simpson, lead singer of boy band the Vamps, meets an 18-year-old fan, while Atomic Kitten's Natasha Hamilton goes on her first date since splitting up with fellow pop star Ritchie Neville, the father of her fourth child. Bernie Clifton, mainstay of 1970s and 1980s TV with his ostrich puppet, has an encounter with an 80-something bellydancer, while rugby league player Keegan Hirst gets an introduction to the gay dating scene from a fitness adviser.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard questions his engineering abilities when he and Sheldon can't get a toy drone to fly. A cancelled flight almost stops Leonard from giving the commencement address at his former high school, while Raj pits his parents against each other when his father cuts him off financially.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: There is a clash of personalities when Sheldon and Leonard's mothers finally meet, while Howard finally decides to do his fair share of the housework - but not without a little help from his friends. Guest starring Christine Baranski and Laurie Metcalf.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt heads to Italy to find out how supermarkets can sell pesto at an affordable price when the traditional ingredients are expensive, while Jimmy Doherty investigates why so many cockles in UK supermarkets come from abroad when he always thought of them as a quintessentially British staple. Kate Quilton takes a trip around Europe's largest marshmallow factory to find out why the sticky treat goes crispy and melty at the same time over the campfire.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin shows Melvyn and Patricia Whitton five properties in Orlando, Florida. They have Â£180,000 to spend on a five-bedroom house for holidays in the sun, where they plan to invite their three daughters and eight grandchildren to join them.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps Steven Weald and Sue Upson search for a winter home north of Naples on Florida's Gulf Coast, and shows the active couple five properties that are within their Â£150,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Entrepreneur and sports fishing enthusiast Ian Walls from Edinburgh has decided to expand his property portfolio by purchasing a place on Grand Bahama where he can spend a couple of months every winter. With his US-based girlfriend Lisa on hand to offer a second opinion, Sara Damergi shows them a selection of homes for sale around his Â£111,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with two pairs of house-hunters. Requiring three bedrooms and space for three cars, as well as their dog, Jodie and Andy finally found what they were looking for in Portsmouth, and although the negotiations didn't work out, that wasn't the end of their story. In Stansted, Phil finds out whether Jocelyn and Simon's dream of a rural idyll was ever completely satisfied.(n)

programme.title: Phil Spencer's Stately Homes
programme.desc: Phil visits Goodwood House in West Sussex, where current owner Lord March opens the doors to the state rooms and takes Phil for a spin round the racetrack.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trainee accountant Annika hosts the first dinner party in Boston, Lincolnshire, where she hopes to impress farmer's wife Carolyn, electrical engineer Adrian, carer Sylwia, and glamorous mum Katy with an evening modelled on a country fayre.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Competitions fanatic Katy hosts the second night in Boston, Lincolnshire, where she aims to impress her guests with her organisation skills and a menu featuring savoury profiteroles. However, fellow contestant Carolyn is critical of the food, and lone male host Adrian struggles to feel included when the conversation turns to hair extensions and fake eyelashes.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the third night of the competition in Boston, Lincolnshire, Adrian hopes that his spicy food and surprises will help him scoop the Â£1,000 prize. Although he was quiet at the table the previous night, Adrian goes on the charm offensive and seems to be impressing his guests - but he may have gone a step too far with his final revelation.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Polish-born carer Sylwia hosts the fourth party of the competition in Boston, Lincolnshire, and hopes her European menu will charm her guests. However, the growing friction between diners Annika and Carolyn threatens to spoil the evening, and the host's chances of winning the Â£1,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final dinner party night in Boston, and the hard-to-please foxhunter Carolyn is planning on using a charm offensive to win over the guests after having criticised each of them during the week. She has offended Annika, called Katy fake, found fault with Sywia's food, and reprimanded Adie, but still believes she may be able to turn things around.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Brigitte Tee, who runs four-star B&B Gorse Meadow in the New Forest, hosts the first round of the competition. Her guests are initially charmed by her eccentric personality, but the discovery of a bathroom window in need of repair and another room containing ladybirds, cobwebs and dust soon takes the shine off their stay.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants head to Lynton in north Devon, where they stay at Alistair and Emma Mundell's four-star Castle Hill Guesthouse. The pair only opened the B&B three months prior to the competition, and hope to impress with their recently renovated rooms, but Brigitte Tee proves hard to win over. Two of their guests are also forced to overcome their fears during a trip on a Victorian cliff railway.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves north to Blackpool and the three-star Vidella B&B run by John Downer and Neil Williams. The pair rig Blackpool-hater Alistair Mundell's room with practical jokes, and take their guests to Funny Girls drag cabaret bar, where Mark Crib confesses he has never had such a surreal time.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Mark Crib welcomes the group to the Urban Beach in Bournemouth, Dorset, for the week's final B&B visit. The boutique establishment features chic rooms with deluxe bathrooms, and the guests approve of the afternoon's activity - a speedboat ride along the Jurassic coast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Brigitte Tee is upset to discover she has been given only one point for cleanliness and a heated exchange results in a walk-out, before the winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Michelle Parsons, an artist and teacher who decided to seize the day and build her own private hideaway in a woodland environment in Essex after she recovered from a serious illness. Together with her architect husband David, Michelle planned to construct a sleek, black-clad, three bedroom house complete with an art studio. However, torrential rain and a potentially disastrous gas leak stalled progress on the build, and as construction gets under way, Kevin wonders whether the couple's decision to eliminate windows on two sides of the property may leave them living in a gloomy bunker.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The 100th episode of the series focuses on the love between patients and their nearest and dearest. Sir John Cockburn, who's 89, arrives at St George's with acute abdominal pain and medics think it could be an abdominal aortic aneurysm and he faces emergency surgery. Meanwhile, 68-year-old Mick and his wife Linda have been involved in a head-on car crash. Mick took the brunt of the impact and has multiple injuries from top to toe that could be life-changing. The couple fell in love after leaving previous relationships, making a happy life together in their mobile home. And 10-year-old Niall is brought to A&E after falling 8ft from a tree on a family picnic. He's broken his arm so badly it looks like a banana, and orthopaedic doctors need to work out how to get it back in place.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old motorcyclist Sam is airlifted to St George's after crashing into a bus and being thrown under a car. He is placed in an induced coma and CT scans reveal Sam has bruising to his lungs, a ruptured kidney, a broken thigh bone and a fractured spine. The priority of the medics is is to stop his internal bleeding, while Sam's parents are rushed to the hospital by police. Meanwhile, seven-year-old Raheem arrives in A&E with his dad Dennis after cutting his wrist while playing at home, and 59-year-old Therese turns up with a painful lump on her finger.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Jack, Dougal and Ted head off for a summer break at a caravan park. However, they immediately get on the wrong side of their fellow holiday-makers, and matters become even more dire when Father Noel Furlong turns up - with his hapless youth group in tow. Comedy, guest starring Graham Norton.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The priests organise a raffle to raise funds to repair their leaky roof, planning to award a car as the prize. Unfortunately, Ted accidentally wrecks it before the grand draw takes place, and embarks on a frantic search for a replacement. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon play the Craggy Island clerics.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with Sean Lock and Sara Pascoe taking on Jon Richardson and Josh Widdicombe. Susie Dent is joined by Alex Horne and the Horne Section in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.(n)

Comedy, guest starring Graham Norton.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and mechanic Fuzz Townshend go to Leeds to repair an AC Aceca, a British sports car that has been kept and loved by its owner Monet since the early 1970s.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to West Sussex to rescue an iconic 1970s motor - and the first truly successful Japanese car export - the mighty Datsun 240z.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to Croydon, south London, to rescue a star car that has been wallowing in its own garage for over 30 years. Its owner, Peter, is a serial petrolhead who has been fixing up motors for friends and family for most of his life, putting his own classic vehicle on the back burner. It's a 1964 Volvo P1800S, one of the most stylish cars to come off any production line, and the vehicle synonymous with cult 1960s TV series The Saint.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In north Surrey, planning enforcement officer Becky is joined by singing teacher Petrina, drum teacher Rob, solicitor Shoba and building company boss Giovanni. Becky plans to delight her guests with an evening of modern home-cooking and a menu including butternut, lamb and a deconstructed apple dessert. The week gets off to an intriguing start with young gun Rob taking a shine to Petrina and vice versa.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Solicitor Shoba hosts the second night in north Surrey, and tries to impress with an Indian-themed menu. However, the guests are less than impressed with the food, especially the main course - a curry made from frozen fish - but will a bit of belly dancing, Bollywood-style, help save the day? Meanwhile, singer Petrina and drummer Rob continue their flirting both at and away from the table.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Drum tutor Rob hosts the third night in north Surrey, hoping his Thai-themed menu will impress his guests, as well as the object of his affections, Petrina. He serves prawns with lemon, lime and chilli for his starter, Thai chicken curry for his main, and rounds off with his own take on an ice-cream sundae. As a single man living in his stepmother's house, Rob thinks his competitors will see him as an underdog, so is intent on pulling out all the stops.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Singing teacher Petrina hosts the fourth dinner party in north Surrey, and she believes her experience of entertaining on cruise ships will give her the edge as she plans a showstopper of an evening. However, the diners seem more interested in their host's continued flirting with fellow guest Rob. Will tonight be the night, or will he shy away when he discovers Petrina has sung in the West End and worked with dozens of household names?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Giovanni hosts the final night of the competition in north Surrey, intent on flying the flag for Sicily with some family inspired recipes. Even with help from his father, a tagliatelle tragedy leaves him with Parmesan on his face. Becky is less than impressed with Giovanni's dishes, and matters go from bad to worse when he attempts to serve his home-made pasta. However, poor food doesn't deter a romantic Rob from popping the question to Petrina, sending shockwaves across the dining room. In a final attempt to bag the Â£1,000, Giovanni invites an opera singer to liven up the proceedings, but his musical initiative may prove too little to save the day.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jon and Julia Shorrock host the first round of the B&B competition from their four-star Derwent House in West Sussex. The guests are split by gender to sample the area, with the men firing machine guns at a shooting range and the women relaxing at a spa - but a carpeted bathroom and shower without hot water raise questions.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Peter and Sharron Sorsky invite their fellow competitors to Kirkby Park B&B in Liverpool, but the presence of a security camera on the outside of the house leaves one guest feeling uneasy. Room inspections uncover all manner of unsightly problems, but a tour of nearby Knowsley Safari Park gives the contestants a chance to enjoy themselves.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Andy and Julian Banner-Price host the competition at their expensive B&B Plas Dinas Country House in Snowdonia, giving their new guests the priciest rooms. Peter and Sharron Sorsky are still reeling from their feedback forms and immediately find fault with the accommodation, and the group is taken to wash bedding the old-fashioned way at the local slate museum.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Annette McAnespie welcomes the group to Hazelwood Farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, for the final B&B visit. The initial impressions are favourable and Peter and Jon begin to bond for the first time during a mosaic class, but not everyone feels up to the planned activity of a cycling tour.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time at Annette McAnespie's farm in Crayke, North Yorkshire, to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances, before the week's winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Guildford, Surrey, the first host is glamorous 22-year-old cosmetic-surgery-fan Shanice, who hopes to show she is more than just a pretty face by serving up sumptuous food and dazzling conversation. The guests she must impress are 32-year-old entertainer David, 69-year-old Norwegian plain-talker Vivi, 47-year-old super-competitive Neil and 25-year-old media sales worker Charlotte. With risky menu choices and a fellow diner's peculiar laughter taking the spotlight, will the conversations ranging from lookalikes to riding polar bears be enough to secure Shanice the prize?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired estate agent Vivi hosts the second dinner party in and around Guildford, and hopes to win with a Norwegian feast, as tensions rise between Shanice and Charlotte.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sales manager Charlotte hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in and around Guildford, hoping to impress her guests enough to win the Â£1,000 prize money. However, she has taken a huge risk with an Asian-inspired menu, which could prove to be too exotic for entertainments manager David.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The penultimate day in and around Guildford, Surrey, sees children's entertainments manager David playing host. Having worked in the entertainments industry all his life, he is no stranger to showing people a good time, and employs his colleagues to put on a performance, while keeping his food simple. Is he forgetting the key ingredients of a dinner party?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final day in and around Guildford, Surrey, sales director Neil devises a sophisticated menu, dismissing his predecessors' efforts as unambitious. However, his unusual fayre is tempered by his choice of yodelling entertainment, which threatens to hinder his chance of scooping the Â£1,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Bolton-based bricklayer Paul Rimmer, who has spent his career restoring and renovating Victorian terrace houses, sets his sights on constructing a state-of-the-art home on a budget of Â£350,000. However, instead of using the construction materials he has spent the past 40 years mastering, he has opted to build his new home from wood. Roping in his wife Carol, daughter Abby and a selection of favoured workmates, Paul and his team begin work on the ultra-modern, five-bedroom build, and Kevin McCloud reveals how extreme weather, exhaustion, and rapidly dwindling funds place the entire project in jeopardy.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics rush to a man who has overdosed on heroin in a public toilet in Bromsgrove, and a 73-year-old man with slurred speech is tested to determine if he has had a stroke.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary following the emergency services in Wiltshire. This edition focuses on the high numbers of crimes of a sexual nature involving women being targeted by predatory males as the county's force receives three times as many reports of voyeurism and men exposing themselves than any other in the UK.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Surgeons must plan their next move carefully when Daisy, a 22-month-old girl, arrives at St George's Hospital by air ambulance after being kicked in the head by a horse at her family's stables. Meanwhile, 54-year-old Mike, who has a form of medication-resistant epilepsy, arrives in minors with a bandage around his jaw after falling face-first onto the pavement. Plus, 100-year-old Florence, who was diagnosed with dementia three years earlier, and her daughter Frances arrive at the hospital when the former suffers what appears to be a stroke.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics rush to a man who has overdosed on heroin in a public toilet in Bromsgrove, and a 73-year-old man with slurred speech is tested to determine if he has had a stroke.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants that he set out to save, including The Mixing Bowl, The Olde Stone Mill, Peter's and Campania. Have their owners pulled themselves out of their nightmares?(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The priests organise a raffle to raise funds to repair their leaky roof, planning to award a car as the prize. Unfortunately, Ted accidentally wrecks it before the grand draw takes place, and embarks on a frantic search for a replacement. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon play the Craggy Island clerics.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps George and Gillian Pook search for a holiday home and a property they can eventually retire to on Costa Rica's Caribbean coast, and shows them places she hopes will be suitable within their Â£100,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Having both retired from the Army, Rachel and Steven Grey are hoping to find the perfect property on Costa Rica's Pacific Costa Ballena where they can run a small B&B. Sara Damergi shows them a variety of possible options for their Â£250,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Leeds and Preston in search of people's junk and clutter to upcycle into valuable new items. Henry makes an international set of clocks out of dashboard time pieces, while Simon builds an eccentric chandelier from car headlights.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Cheshire and the Wirral, where items include a 1920s dance hall lighting system, an antique butter churn and an old enamel advertising sign.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Donna and Paul move into their new home in western France, but struggle with the language, while Adam from Warwickshire is hoping to join the band of expat chateau owners.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Bell House Hotel in Wiltshire, where business partners Chris Williams and Jacqui Hogg hope guests will appreciate their attempts to bring London chic to the shires. Susan and David Speak are frustrated by the low shower pressure, while Jade Hendon butts heads with Matthew over healthy eating at dinner and the hosts' absence at breakfast the next morning gets the criticism flowing.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Partridge in Oxfordshire, where young couple Matthew Cockman and Paula Szkwarkowska hope guests will get on board with their passion for local organic food. A communal bathroom has the guests lost for words, and the lack of a hot breakfast included in the price leaves a bad taste in everyone's mouths.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Strawberry Gardens B&B in Heysham for the third day of the contest, where husband and wife team Susan and David Speak offer home-from-home accommodation and an abundance of northern hospitality. Sports enthusiast David treats his guests to a traditional darts competition, adjudicated by former world champion Alan Warriner-Little, but he is unimpressed when Paula sends back her evening meal. However, it is Chris and Jacqui's feedback that disappoints the hosts the most.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Bateman's Mill near Chesterfield, where manager Jade Hendon has high hopes of being the best. But she faces an uphill battle when David and Susan go out of their way to find hairs in the bathroom - and to make mattters worse, her Derbyshire breakfast is not a hit with Chris and Jacqui.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores, starting with Chris and Jacqui, owners of the Bell House, who want to find out why Matthew marked them down for being 'too tall' for his bed. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple race to open a shabby-chic B&B and restaurant in France, hoping their high-end food will go down a treat on their launch weekend. Elsewhere, two stargazers renovate a new observatory and a family running a bar on the Costa Blanca attempt to deal with a series of setbacks.(n)

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Melanie Sykes hosts as 12 amateur potters compete against one another, with judges Keith Brymer Jones and Sue Pryke asking the contenders to throw a breakfast set and then egg cups.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Documentary following Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree through the process of buying and restoring a French chateau that has been empty for the past four decades. Their new home has 12 acres of land, nine outbuildings, a walled garden and a moat - but it also has no electricity, heating or water, and proves to be a far more complicated DIY project than expected.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Simon and Jasmine Dale take on the challenging task of building a 21st-century, low-impact three-bedroom family home, starting the build with only Â£500 in the bank. As part of a pioneering, government-backed, sustainable village called Lammas, the couple must adhere to their fierce planning conditions of self-sufficiency or risk losing everything.(n)

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Documentary charting the step-by-step process of the construction of impressive vehicles, beginning by following the making of a new Â£170,000 Continental GT in the Bentley factory. From the design teams to the craftsmen in the wood shop, this is the definitive guide of how to build a motor car.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Rob Beckett take on guest team captain Alan Carr and Sarah Millican in the classic words-and-numbers quiz, while Nick Mohammed joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Ninety-one-year-old Cyril is airlifted to hospital after a car crash which required him to be cut from his car. Meanwhile, the medical team treat 57-year-old Caroline, who arrives at St George's with chest pains, plus five-year-old Roman has a pencil stuck in his ear.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: The Hurting
programme.desc: Clip show featuring the voice of Jake Yapp, as home videos reveal the dumbest things attempted by adults, kids and even animals.(n)

programme.title: The Hurting

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: Series following Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald who earn a living by restoring forgotten relics to their former glory.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Frank falls ill, so Mike's brother Robbie takes his place on the latest expedition, which leads to a set of antique spurs that could be worth a fortune.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Robbie sort through a property full of rusty relics and attend a lumberjack contest.(n)

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Celebrities undertake some of the most perilous journeys in the world. Comedians Ed Byrne and Andy Parsons drive along Siberia's M56 Kolyma highway, known as the 'Road of Bones'. Along the way, they brave sub-zero temperatures, traverse icy routes, encounter deserted gulags and meet the locals who inhabit the frozen wilderness, but despite picking up practical tips for survival, the duo feel they are out of their depth.(n)

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Broadcasters Sue Perkins and Liza Tarbuck drive along the Ho Chi Minh Trail, a network of roads and paths in Southeast Asia that was littered with unexploded bombs following the Vietnam War. Their journey takes them through jungles and mountains to some of the least-explored corners of Laos and Vietnam, and proves to be a gruelling test of their bravery, motoring skills and senses of humour.(n)

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and David Baddiel travel along more than 1,200 miles of hazardous roads in Ethiopia on a journey to Aksum, the country's holiest place. However, they have to contend with more than fatigue and unreliable terrain to reach their destination, as they encounter drug-taking truck drivers, suffer a disastrous breakdown and start to run out of fuel at the worst possible time.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond compete in a land race from Resolute, Canada, to the North Pole in their most ambitious challenge to date. Pitting horse power against dog power they travel 450 miles across mountainous land masses and jagged sea ice at temperatures as low as minus 65C.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Presenters Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are challenged to build a bridge over the River Kwai in Thailand. In order to do that, however, they have to drive across Burma, a country that has been largely closed to Westerners for more than 40 years, and must make the trip in three lorries, each bought sight unseen from the internet on a limited budget. What follows is an epic journey of beautiful scenery, regular adversity, ongoing malfunction and the constant bickering of the three men.(n)

programme.title: Room 101

programme.title: Medieval Murder Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Amy Dudley: The Queen, Her Lover, and His Wife
programme.desc: The suspicious death of Elizabethan noblewoman Amy Dudley, the wife of Robert Dudley, the favourite and alleged lover of Queen Elizabeth I and the man the monarch is said to have wanted to marry - if only he had been available. Amy's body was found at the foot of the stairs at Cumnor Place, her home in Oxfordshire in 1560, and her death caused a scandal - with the finger of blame pointed firmly at Dudley.(n)

programme.title: Medieval Murder Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Princes in The Tower: Royal Blood
programme.desc: Examining the case of the princes in the tower, and questioning whether or not Richard III ordered their deaths.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The German police's attempts to catch a depraved and psychotic killer, who struck fear into women travelling on Berlin's S-Bahn train network during the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Expert hauliers Eric Harrison and Chris Case move an iconic Deltic diesel, better known as The Royal Highland Fusilier, halfway across the country to the Telford Steam Fair.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1970s Bond Bug three-wheeler divides opinion among Mathewsons customers, while a retired engineer reluctantly auctions off his late father's treasured 60s Jaguar Mark 2.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Mathewsons agree to sell a vintage threshing machine and large collection of tractors, but extracting them from old barns on a Yorkshire farm proves a bigger job than anticipated.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A Cold War Soviet classic goes under the hammer. Built like a tank, the Moskovitch gets few admirers for style, but finds a loving home with a fan of Eastern Bloc classics.(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the region's islands, which are home to a vast array of extraordinary wildlife. The programme reveals how remote human communities native to the area are surrounded by harsh terrain and subjected to extreme weather conditions. Benedict Cumberbatch narrates.(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: A look at how the 20,000 islands in the region became colonised by humans and animals. For the Polynesians, the Pacific's first people, their journeys constituted a feat of navigational ingenuity, while animals often had to rely on violent forces of nature, such as cyclones and tsunamis, to reach the shores.(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: An exploration of the marine wildlife of the region. Included are the epic journeys of sperm whales from one side of the ocean to the other in search of food and a mate, the thriving species that inhabit New Zealand's coast, and the tropical penguins and sea lions supported by the Galapagos Islands.(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe narrates the first of three special editions of the archive music show focusing on 1960s hits, beginning with tracks by artists including the Foundations, Julie Driscoll, Sandie Shaw and Procol Harum.(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Another selection of performances from the archives, focusing on hits of the 1960s. Featured artists include Clodagh Rodgers, the Move, Tom Jones and the Bee Gees, plus more from Top of the Pops and other shows from the era. Narrated by Mark Radcliffe.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris attempts to cross the length of Cuba, the only island in the Caribbean to boast an extensive railway network, starting in Havana in the west. This proves to be a major challenge given the unreliability of the service and the old trains regularly used, but makes for a fascinating journey to Chris's ultimate goal, the American naval base at Guantanamo Bay. Along the way he soaks up the sights and sounds of Havana, retraces the footsteps of the country's national hero Che Guevara, and visits the ghost town of Camilo Cienfuegos - a relic from Cuba's colonial sugar plantation era.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris journeys a thousand miles across Japan, starting out in Nagasaki, where the story of eastern railways began 150 years ago with the help of an enterprising Brit. In Fukuoka, he visits one of the Bullet Train's giant repair centres and in Hiroshima, he unravels what happened to its streetcar network after the world's first atomic bomb devastated the city. Along the way, Chris also meets a superstar cat and a singing conductor, while also checking into a popular trainspotter hotel and riding a steam-powered mountain railway.(n)

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Heavy haulage experts Ian Bagladi and Jamie Turnbull need to move a classical but abandoned 63-year-old, 70-tonne locomotive, through the English countryside.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The Falkirk wheel is the first and only rotating boat lift, capable of moving 600 tonnes of boat and water over a 25-metre height difference in just minutes.(n)
